check_logic.condition: If the sign Scorpio occupies the 7th house.
check_logic.calculation: Check the sign occupying the 7th house in the birth chart. If it is Scorpio, this condition is met.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific strength enhancement for Scorpio in the 7th house, as described by this rule, will not apply.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Verse 17
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the Sanskrit text 'जलाश्रयो वृश्चिकनामधेयः ससप्तमस्थानगतो बली स्यात्'. The English translation provided is incomplete and partially unclear for the first part of the verse, hence only the clear part is extracted.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength or placement of a planet or Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if a planet or Lagna falls in the concluding portion of Kataka (Cancer), Vrischika (Scorpio), or Meena (Pisces). The text defines Riksha-Sandhi as the concluding portion of these signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary or Lagna strength/placement, leading to misinterpretation of results due to overlooking a weak or sensitive point.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 22
rules.extraction_notes: Definition of Riksha-Sandhi from the text. The exact 'concluding portion' (e.g., last degree, last few degrees) is not specified in this verse.

rules.rule_name: Planetary placement in Lame Signs (Makara & Dhanus)

check_logic.condition: When a planet is placed in Makara (Capricorn) or Dhanus (Sagittarius).
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet's sign is Makara or Dhanus. These signs are described as 'lame' (चपहू).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to account for the inherent 'lameness' of the sign, potentially leading to an overestimation of the planet's ability to act or move effectively, or indicating some form of impediment in the areas signified by the planet/house.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 22
rules.extraction_notes: Attribute of Makara and Dhanus signs mentioned in the verse.

rules.rule_name: Fatal effect of Makara/Dhanus Lagna during twilight birth

check_logic.condition: If the birth occurs during the two periods of twilight (Sandhya Kala) AND the Lagna (Ascendant) is Makara (Capricorn) or Dhanus (Sagittarius).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the birth time falls within the defined twilight periods (e.g., sunrise/sunset transition). Check if the calculated Lagna is Makara or Dhanus.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify a potentially fatal or extremely inauspicious birth condition, leading to fundamentally incorrect life predictions or a severe misjudgment of the native's initial life circumstances.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 22
rules.extraction_notes: Direct statement about Makara and Dhanus being 'fatal' (नाशकरौ) in twilight periods.

check_logic.condition: If a biped sign occupies the Lagna (1st house).
check_logic.calculation: Identify biped signs: Gemini (Mithuna), Libra (Thula), Aquarius (Kumbha), Virgo (Kanya), and the fore part of Sagittarius (Dhanus). Check if the Lagna falls in any of these signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced strength of the Lagna, affecting the native's overall well-being, personality, and general significations of the 1st house.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'नृयुक तुला घटः कन्या पूर्वमर्द्ध च धन्विनः । लग्नस्था वलिना नित्यमेते हि नरराशयः ॥'

rules.rule_name: Biped Sign Strength in Kendra during Day

check_logic.condition: If a biped sign occupies a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th) during the day.
check_logic.calculation: Identify biped signs: Gemini (Mithuna), Libra (Thula), Aquarius (Kumbha), Virgo (Kanya), and the fore part of Sagittarius (Dhanus). Check if any of these signs are in a Kendra house. Determine if the birth time is during the day (sunrise to sunset).

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced strength of the Kendra house, affecting its significations related to stability, happiness, relationships, and career.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'केन्द्रं गतोह्नि द्विपदो बलाढ्यः' and 'नृयुक तुला घटः कन्या पूर्वमर्द्ध च धन्विनः । लग्नस्था वलिना नित्यमेते हि नरराशयः ॥'

check_logic.condition: If a quadruped sign occupies the 10th house.
check_logic.calculation: Identify quadruped signs: Aries (Aja), Taurus (Go), Leo (Simha), and the latter part of Sagittarius (Dhanus). Check if the 10th house falls in any of these signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced strength of the 10th house, affecting career, public image, and achievements.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'धनुषोऽर्द्धाजगोसिंहा बलिनः खे चतुष्पदाः'

rules.rule_name: Quadruped Sign Strength in Kendra during Night

check_logic.condition: If a quadruped sign occupies a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th) during the night.
check_logic.calculation: Identify quadruped signs: Aries (Aja), Taurus (Go), Leo (Simha), and the latter part of Sagittarius (Dhanus). Check if any of these signs are in a Kendra house. Determine if the birth time is during the night (sunset to sunrise).

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced strength of the Kendra house, affecting its significations related to stability, happiness, relationships, and career.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'चतुष्पदाः केन्द्रगता रजन्याम्' and 'धनुषोऽर्द्धाजगोसिंहा बलिनः खे चतुष्पदाः'

check_logic.condition: If a watery sign occupies the 4th house.
check_logic.calculation: Identify watery signs: Cancer (Kataka), Pisces (Meena), and the latter half of Capricorn (Makara). Check if the 4th house falls in any of these signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced strength of the 4th house, affecting significations related to home, mother, happiness, and property.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'चतुर्थे कर्कटो मीनो मकरार्द्ध च पश्चिमे । विशेया बलिनो नित्यमेते हि जलराशयः ।'

check_logic.condition: If Scorpio (Vrischika) occupies the 7th house.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the 7th house falls in the sign of Scorpio (Vrischika).

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced strength of the 7th house, affecting significations related to marriage, partnerships, and public dealings.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'सप्तमे वृश्चिकः कीटो बलवान् परिकीर्तितः ।'

rules.rule_name: Reptile/Aquatic Sign Strength in Kendra during Junction Periods

check_logic.condition: If a reptile/aquatic sign occupies a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th) during junction periods (morning/evening twilight).
check_logic.calculation: Identify reptile/aquatic signs: Scorpio (Vrischika), Cancer (Kataka), Pisces (Meena), and the latter half of Capricorn (Makara). Check if any of these signs are in a Kendra house. Determine if the birth time is during a junction period (e.g., 1 hour before/after sunrise/sunset).

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced strength of the Kendra house, affecting its significations related to stability, happiness, relationships, and career.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'कीटास्तु सर्वे यदि कंटकस्याः सन्धिद्वये वीर्ययुता भवन्ति' and the note 'The term कीयः (reptiles) here includes not only Vrischika Rasi, but also the other aquatic signs.'

rules.rule_name: Sign State - Blind at Dead of Night

check_logic.condition: When a planet or house falls in Mesha (Aries), Vrishabha (Taurus), or Simha (Leo), and the event or query relates to the dead of night.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign and the relevant time of day (dead of night).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the clarity, awareness, or effectiveness associated with the significations of the sign/house/planet during that specific time.
rules.extraction_notes: Describes a state of 'blindness' for specific signs during the dead of night (Mahanishandha).

check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign and the relevant time of day (midday).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the clarity, awareness, or effectiveness associated with the significations of the sign/house/planet during that specific time.

check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign and the relevant time of day (forenoon).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the receptiveness, responsiveness, or communication associated with the significations of the sign/house/planet during that specific time.

check_logic.condition: Native's birth occurs in a Gandanta zone.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the specific degrees/nakshatras that constitute Gandanta (junctions of water and fire signs, or specific nakshatra endings/beginnings). Check if the Moon or Lagna falls within these zones.

rules.impact_if_violated: Extreme outcomes, potentially non-survival, enmity towards mother, destruction of family, or conversely, great kingship if survived.

cancellation_conditions.condition: Native survives the Gandanta period (e.g., early childhood).
cancellation_conditions.impact: Negative effects are mitigated, and positive effects (like becoming a king) may manifest.

rules.classical_reference: Saravali (as cited in Jataka Parijata Verse 23 notes)
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the notes section of Jataka Parijata Verse 23, citing Saravali regarding the effects of birth in Kakshasandhi (Gandanta).

rules.rule_name: Sign Strength Determines Quantity of Signified Articles

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the quantity or abundance of articles/materials signified by the sign, leading to inaccurate predictions regarding availability or lack.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the statement 'their quantity being small or great, according as the signs typifying them are weak or strong.' The specific method for calculating sign strength is not detailed in this verse but is implied as a prerequisite for quantitative assessment.

rules.rule_name: Sign Gender Classification: Odd Signs are Male

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of sign gender will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, especially concerning disposition and specific yogas.

check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is a fixed sign (Vrishabha, Simha, Vrischika, Kumbha)

rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Satyacharya's statement 'चरसंज्ञाः स्थिरसंज्ञाः द्विःप्रकृतिरिति राशयः क्रमशः'

check_logic.condition: When classifying the modality of a sign
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is a dual sign (Mithuna, Kanya, Dhanus, Meena)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of sign modality will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting predictions related to stability, change, and dual nature.

check_logic.condition: When classifying the length of a sign
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is a short sign (Meena, Mesha, Kumbha)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of sign length will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting interpretations related to duration and manifestation.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Saravali's statement 'हस्वास्तिमिगाजघटा मिथुनधनुःककिंमृगमुखाश्च समाः वृश्चिककन्या मृगपतिवणिजो दीर्घाः समाख्याताः'. Timi=Meena, Aja=Mesha, Ghata=Kumbha.

check_logic.condition: When classifying the length of a sign
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is a medium sign (Mithuna, Dhanus, Karka, Simha)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of sign length will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting interpretations related to duration and manifestation.

Note: Simha also appears in the 'Long Signs' classification in the same text, indicating a potential textual variation or specific context not fully captured.

check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is a long sign (Vrischika, Kanya, Simha, Tula)

Note: Simha also appears in the 'Medium Signs' classification in the same text, indicating a potential textual variation or specific context not fully captured.

rules.rule_name: Sign Strength by Time of Day: Night-Strong Signs

check_logic.condition: When assessing the temporal strength (Dig Bala) of a sign
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is one of Vrishabha, Mithuna, Karka, Dhanus, Mesha, Makara

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of sign strength by time of day will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting the evaluation of planetary strength and house significations.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata 14, Parashara Jataka, Shambhu Hora Prakash
rules.extraction_notes: Confirmed by multiple sources in the text. Jataka Parijata Sloka 14: 'Vrishabha, Mithuna, Kataka, Dhanus Mesha and Makara possess strength at night.' Shambhu Hora Prakash: 'मेषो वृषद्वन्द्रकुलरिचापकुरङ्गवस्त्राश्च निशाबलाः स्युः'.

rules.rule_name: Sign Strength by Time of Day: Day-Strong Signs

check_logic.condition: When assessing the temporal strength (Dig Bala) of a sign
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is one of Simha, Kanya, Tula, Vrischika, Kumbha, Meena (the remaining signs not strong at night)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of sign strength by time of day will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting the evaluation of planetary strength and house significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Jataka Parijata Sloka 14: 'The rest appear with their heads and have strength during the day.'

rules.rule_name: Sign Rising Type Classification: Prishtodaya Signs

check_logic.condition: When classifying the rising type of a sign
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is a Prishtodaya sign (Vrishabha, Karka, Dhanus, Mesha, Makara)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of sign rising type will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting interpretations related to manifestation and timing.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Jataka Parijata Sloka 14: 'These excepting the sign Mithuna rise with their rear portion first.' This refers to the night-strong signs (Vrishabha, Mithuna, Karka, Dhanus, Mesha, Makara) minus Mithuna.

rules.rule_name: Sign Rising Type Classification: Shirshodaya Signs

check_logic.condition: When classifying the rising type of a sign
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign is a Shirshodaya sign (Mithuna, Simha, Kanya, Tula, Vrischika, Kumbha)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of sign rising type will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting interpretations related to manifestation and timing.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Jataka Parijata Sloka 14: 'The rest appear with their heads'. This refers to the signs not classified as Prishtodaya or Ubhayodaya.

rules.rule_name: Sign Rising Type Classification: Ubhayodaya Sign (Meena)

check_logic.condition: When classifying the rising type of the sign Meena (Pisces)
check_logic.calculation: Meena is classified as an Ubhayodaya sign (rises with both head and rear portions)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of Meena's rising type will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting interpretations related to manifestation and timing.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Jataka Parijata Sloka 14: 'The rising sign at any given time, if represented by a couple of fish figures is to come under both the Prishtodaya (पृष्ठोदय) and Seershodaya (शीषर्योदय).'

rules.rule_name: Sign Strength based on Rising Type and Time of Day: Shirshodaya Day Strength

check_logic.condition: When assessing the temporal strength (Dig Bala) of a Shirshodaya sign
check_logic.calculation: Shirshodaya signs are considered strong during the day (दिवा)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of sign strength based on rising type and time of day will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis, affecting the evaluation of planetary strength and house significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Parashara Jataka: 'दिवा शीर्योदयाश्चैव सन्ध्यायामुभयोदयाः। नक्तं पृष्ठोदयाश्चैव बलाधिक्या उदीरिताः ॥'

rules.rule_name: Rasi composition by Nakshatra quarters - Mesha

check_logic.condition: When determining the precise position of a planet or point within the Mesha Rasi based on Nakshatra divisions.
check_logic.calculation: Mesha Rasi comprises all four quarters of Aswini, all four quarters of Bharani, and the first quarter of Krittika Nakshatra.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Rasi-Nakshatra mapping leads to erroneous planetary positions and subsequent analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 8
rules.extraction_notes: Mapping of Nakshatra quarters to Rasi as per classical text.

check_logic.calculation: Identify if the body part is the chest, or if the location/nature aligns with fields, wells, riverbanks, temples, or pleasant resorts.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body part, location, or nature of person/object.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1, subsequent verses to Sl. 10-12
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Sanskrit verse describing Cancer.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body part, location, or nature of person/object.

rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Sanskrit verse describing Leo.

check_logic.condition: When assessing Moon's dignity and strength
check_logic.calculation: Check if Moon is in Taurus (Vrishabha Rashi) and its longitude is between 0 and 3 degrees.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Moon's inherent strength and beneficence, leading to flawed predictions regarding emotional well-being, mother, mind, and general prosperity.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 27
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'वृषादिभागत्रयमुच्चमिन्दोः' (The first three parts of Taurus are Moon's exaltation).

check_logic.condition: When assessing Moon's dignity and strength
check_logic.calculation: Check if Moon is in Taurus (Vrishabha Rashi) and its longitude is between 3 and 30 degrees.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Moon's inherent strength and beneficence, leading to flawed predictions regarding emotional well-being, mother, mind, and general prosperity.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 27
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'मूलत्रिकोणं परतस्तु सर्वम्' (The rest of Taurus is Moolatrikona). Moon's degree must be greater than 3.0 and less than or equal to 30.0 in Taurus.

check_logic.condition: When assessing Mars' dignity and strength
check_logic.calculation: Check if Mars is in Aries (Mesha Rashi) and its longitude is between 0 and 12 degrees.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Mars' inherent strength and beneficence, leading to flawed predictions regarding courage, siblings, property, and aggression.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 27
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'मेषादिका द्वादशभागसंज्ञाः कुजस्य कोणं' (The first twelve parts of Aries are Mars' Moolatrikona).

rules.rule_name: Mars Own Sign in Aries (12-30 degrees)

check_logic.condition: When assessing Mars' dignity and strength
check_logic.calculation: Check if Mars is in Aries (Mesha Rashi) and its longitude is between 12 and 30 degrees.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Mars' inherent strength and beneficence, leading to flawed predictions regarding courage, siblings, property, and aggression.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 27
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'परतः स्वर्भ स्यात्' (The rest is own sign). Mars' degree must be greater than 12.0 and less than or equal to 30.0 in Aries.

rules.rule_name: Impact of Sheershodaya Lagna on Outcomes

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for success of an initiated action or the general fortune of a native's endeavors.
check_logic.calculation: If the Lagna (Ascendant) at the time of query/birth is a Sheershodaya sign, it indicates success for desired actions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misjudgment of the potential for success or failure of an undertaking.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 15
rules.extraction_notes: Direct implication from the sloka: 'शीषर्षोदये समाभवांछितकार्यसिद्धिः'

rules.rule_name: Impact of Prishtodaya Lagna on Outcomes

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for failure of an initiated action or the general misfortune of a native's endeavors.
check_logic.calculation: If the Lagna (Ascendant) at the time of query/birth is a Prishtodaya sign, it indicates failure and loss of strength for actions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misjudgment of the potential for success or failure of an undertaking.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 15
rules.extraction_notes: Direct implication from the sloka: 'पृष्ठोदये विफलता बलविद्रवश्च'

check_logic.condition: When assessing planetary strength or suitability of a time for an action.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is strong during the day (Dinabali) or night (Naktaviryavan) based on classical texts.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary strength and its influence on outcomes, especially for Muhurta.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 15
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'शस्तं दिवा दिनवले निशि नक्तवीर्ये रात्रौ विपर्ययबले गमनं न शस्तम्'

rules.rule_name: Action Suitability based on Planetary Diurnal/Nocturnal Strength

check_logic.condition: When determining the auspiciousness of initiating an action, especially travel.
check_logic.calculation: If a planet governing the action is strong during the day (Dinabali), the action is auspicious during the day. If strong during the night (Naktaviryavan), auspicious during the night. Actions (like travel) are inauspicious at night for day-strong planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Selection of inauspicious timing for actions, leading to failure or adverse results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 15
rules.extraction_notes: Direct implication from 'शस्तं दिवा दिनवले निशि नक्तवीर्ये रात्रौ विपर्ययबले गमनं न शस्तम्'

rules.rule_name: Hora Lordship for Janma Kundali (Satya/Varahamihira System)

check_logic.condition: When calculating Hora (D2) lords for a birth chart (Janma Kundali).
check_logic.calculation: For an odd sign (Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius, Aquarius), the 1st Hora is ruled by the Sun and the 2nd Hora by the Moon. For an even sign (Taurus, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, Pisces), the 1st Hora is ruled by the Moon and the 2nd Hora by the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Hora (D2) chart generation, leading to erroneous analysis of wealth, family, and sustenance.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 31
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is explicitly stated as the one recognized for 'horoscopy' or 'जातके' (birth chart), distinguishing it from the Yavanas' system used for Prasna.

rules.rule_name: Drekkana Lordship for Janma Kundali (Satya/Varahamihira System)

check_logic.condition: When calculating Drekkana (D3) lords for a birth chart (Janma Kundali).
check_logic.calculation:
The lords of the three Drekkanas are determined by the Lagna lord, 5th lord, and 9th lord, based on the sign type:
- Movable signs (Chara Rashi): 1st Drekkana Lord = Lagna Lord, 2nd Drekkana Lord = 5th Lord, 3rd Drekkana Lord = 9th Lord.
- Fixed signs (Sthira Rashi): 1st Drekkana Lord = 9th Lord, 2nd Drekkana Lord = Lagna Lord, 3rd Drekkana Lord = 5th Lord.
- Dual signs (Dwiswabhava Rashi): 1st Drekkana Lord = 5th Lord, 2nd Drekkana Lord = 9th Lord, 3rd Drekkana Lord = Lagna Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Drekkana (D3) chart generation, leading to erroneous analysis of siblings, courage, and efforts.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 31
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is explicitly stated for 'जातके' (horoscopy), distinguishing it from the system used for Prasna.

check_logic.condition: When calculating Saptamamsa (D7) lords for any house or planet.
check_logic.calculation: For an odd sign, the Saptamamsa lords are counted regularly from the lord of that sign (e.g., 1st Saptamamsa lord is the sign lord, 2nd is the lord of the 2nd sign from it, etc.). For an even sign, the Saptamamsa lords are reckoned from the lord of the 7th house from that sign onwards (e.g., 1st Saptamamsa lord is the lord of the 7th sign from it, 2nd is the lord of the 8th sign from it, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Saptamamsa (D7) chart generation, leading to erroneous analysis of children, progeny, and marital happiness.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 31
rules.extraction_notes: This rule provides the fundamental method for calculating Saptamamsa lords based on the parity of the sign.

rules.rule_name: Divisional System Selection for Janma vs. Prasna

check_logic.condition: Before performing any divisional chart calculations (e.g., Hora, Drekkana).
check_logic.calculation: Identify whether the chart being analyzed is a Janma Kundali (birth chart) or a Prasna Kundali (horary chart). Use the Satya/Varahamihira convention for Hora and Drekkana calculations for Janma Kundali. Use the Yavanas' convention for Hora and Drekkana calculations for Prasna Kundali.

rules.impact_if_violated: Applying the incorrect system for divisional chart calculations will lead to fundamentally flawed charts and completely erroneous predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 31
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is a meta-validation, dictating which set of calculation rules to apply based on the type of astrological query (natal vs. horary).

check_logic.condition: When calculating divisional charts or referring to the whole sign.
check_logic.calculation: Lagna represents the whole of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect understanding of the fundamental unit of a Rasi, leading to erroneous chart calculations.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Lagna as the whole sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Hora (D2) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Hora represents 1/2 (half) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Hora chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of wealth and well-being.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Hora as half of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Drekkana (D3) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Drekkana represents 1/3 (one-third) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Drekkana chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of siblings and courage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Drekkana as one-third of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Swaramsa (D7) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Swaramsa represents 1/7 (one-seventh) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Swaramsa chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of children.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Swaramsa as one-seventh of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa (D9) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Navamsa represents 1/6 (one-sixth) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Navamsa chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of marriage and dharma. Note: Classical texts generally define Navamsa as 1/9th of a sign. This text states 1/6th.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Text explicitly states Navamsa as 1/6th, which is a known discrepancy with other classical texts that define it as 1/9th.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Dasamamsa (D10) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Dasamamsa represents 1/10 (one-tenth) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dasamamsa chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of career and status.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Dasamamsa as one-tenth of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Dwadasamsa (D12) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Dwadasamsa represents 1/12 (one-twelfth) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dwadasamsa chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of parents and heritage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Dwadasamsa as one-twelfth of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Kalamsa (D16) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Kalamsa represents 1/16 (one-sixteenth) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Kalamsa chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of vehicles and happiness.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Kalamsa as one-sixteenth of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Thrimsamsa (D30) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Thrimsamsa represents 1/30 (one-thirtieth) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Thrimsamsa chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of misfortunes and general well-being.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Thrimsamsa as one-thirtieth of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Shashtyamsa (D60) divisional chart.
check_logic.calculation: Shashtyamsa represents 1/60 (one-sixtieth) of a Rasi (sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Shashtyamsa chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of all general matters and past karma.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Shashtyamsa as one-sixtieth of a sign.

check_logic.condition: When referring to the group of ten principal divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: The group of Lagna, Hora, Drekkana, Swaramsa, Navamsa, Dasamamsa, Dwadasamsa, Kalamsa, Thrimsamsa, and Shashtyamsa is usually called Dasavarga (दशवर्ग).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misunderstanding of astrological terminology and the comprehensive system of divisional charts.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the collective term 'Dasavarga'.

check_logic.condition: When determining the lord of Hora divisions within an odd sign.
check_logic.calculation: In an odd sign, the first half (Hora) belongs to the Sun, and the second half (Hora) belongs to the Moon.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Hora chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of wealth and well-being.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assigning Hora lordship based on sign parity.

check_logic.condition: When determining the lord of Hora divisions within an even sign.
check_logic.calculation: In an even sign, the first half (Hora) belongs to the Moon, and the second half (Hora) belongs to the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Hora chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of wealth and well-being.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assigning Hora lordship based on sign parity.

check_logic.condition: When determining the lord of the first Drekkana (0-10 degrees) of a sign.
check_logic.calculation: The first Drekkana of a sign is owned by the lord of the sign itself.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Drekkana chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of siblings and courage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assigning Drekkana lordship.

check_logic.condition: When determining the lord of the second Drekkana (10-20 degrees) of a sign.
check_logic.calculation: The second Drekkana of a sign is owned by the lord of the 5th house from that sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Drekkana chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of siblings and courage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assigning Drekkana lordship.

check_logic.condition: When determining the lord of the third Drekkana (20-30 degrees) of a sign.
check_logic.calculation: The third Drekkana of a sign is owned by the lord of the 9th house from that sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Drekkana chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of siblings and courage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 30
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assigning Drekkana lordship.

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Starting Sign Determination for Fiery Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa (D9) position for a planet or Lagna located in a Fiery Sign (Aries, Leo, Sagittarius).
check_logic.calculation: For planets or Lagna in Aries (Mesha), Leo (Simha), or Sagittarius (Dhanus), the Navamsa counting sequence begins from Aries (Mesha).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of planetary and Lagna positions in the Navamsa (D9) chart, rendering all D9 analysis invalid and leading to erroneous predictions, especially concerning marriage, dharma, and general fortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 33
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting sign for Navamsa calculation based on the natal sign's element (Fiery).

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Starting Sign Determination for Earthy Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa (D9) position for a planet or Lagna located in an Earthy Sign (Taurus, Virgo, Capricorn).
check_logic.calculation: For planets or Lagna in Taurus (Vrishabha), Virgo (Kanya), or Capricorn (Makara), the Navamsa counting sequence begins from Capricorn (Makara).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of planetary and Lagna positions in the Navamsa (D9) chart, rendering all D9 analysis invalid and leading to erroneous predictions, especially concerning marriage, dharma, and general fortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 33
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting sign for Navamsa calculation based on the natal sign's element (Earthy).

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Starting Sign Determination for Airy Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa (D9) position for a planet or Lagna located in an Airy Sign (Gemini, Libra, Aquarius).
check_logic.calculation: For planets or Lagna in Gemini (Mithuna), Libra (Thula), or Aquarius (Kumbha), the Navamsa counting sequence begins from Libra (Thula).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of planetary and Lagna positions in the Navamsa (D9) chart, rendering all D9 analysis invalid and leading to erroneous predictions, especially concerning marriage, dharma, and general fortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 33
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting sign for Navamsa calculation based on the natal sign's element (Airy).

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Starting Sign Determination for Watery Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa (D9) position for a planet or Lagna located in a Watery Sign (Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces).
check_logic.calculation: For planets or Lagna in Cancer (Kataka), Scorpio (Vrischika), or Pisces (Meena), the Navamsa counting sequence begins from Cancer (Kataka).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of planetary and Lagna positions in the Navamsa (D9) chart, rendering all D9 analysis invalid and leading to erroneous predictions, especially concerning marriage, dharma, and general fortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 33
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting sign for Navamsa calculation based on the natal sign's element (Watery).

rules.rule_name: Minimum Divisional Charts for General Analysis

check_logic.condition: When performing a general astrological analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Count the number of divisional charts (Vargas) consulted. These typically include Rasi, Hora, Drekkana, Navamsa, Dwadasamsa, Trimsamsa, and sometimes Sapthamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: The astrological analysis will be incomplete and may lack the necessary depth for accurate predictions, as crucial areas of life are not examined through their specific divisional charts.
rules.extraction_notes: Refers to Shadvarga (6 Vargas) and Sapthavarga (7 Vargas) as commonly resorted to.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the fundamental strength (Maha) of a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is situated in its own sign (Sva Varga) in at least five specific divisional charts (Vargas). The specific 5 Vargas are not explicitly listed in this excerpt but are implied by 'Panchavarga'.

rules.impact_if_violated: The assessment of a planet's fundamental strength will be incorrect, leading to erroneous conclusions about its capacity to deliver results, especially if it is mistakenly considered 'Maha' or its lack of 'Maha' status is overlooked.
rules.classical_reference: Garga, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: The phrase 'पश्चवर्गगतोऽप्येवं महो भवति नान्यथा' directly translates to 'a planet in its own Varga in five Vargas becomes strong, not otherwise'.

rules.rule_name: Rasi Chart for Body and Self Analysis

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the native's physical body, general constitution, or overall self.
check_logic.calculation: The Rasi (Lagna) chart must be consulted for this analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Analysis of the native's physical well-being and general life aspects will be inaccurate or incomplete without consulting the Rasi chart.
rules.classical_reference: Horapradeepa, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'लग्नाद्देहविचारो'.

rules.rule_name: Hora Chart for Wealth and Assets Analysis

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the native's wealth, financial status, and assets.
check_logic.calculation: The Hora chart must be consulted for this analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions regarding financial prosperity and accumulation of assets will be unreliable without consulting the Hora chart.
rules.classical_reference: Horapradeepa, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'होरातश्वार्थसंपदो शेयाः'.

rules.rule_name: Drekkana Chart for Karma Results Analysis

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the results or fruits of the native's actions (karma).
check_logic.calculation: The Drekkana chart must be consulted for this analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Understanding of the karmic outcomes and their manifestation in the native's life will be incomplete without consulting the Drekkana chart.
rules.classical_reference: Horapradeepa, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'ग्रेकाणात्कर्म फलं'.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing matters related to siblings.
check_logic.calculation: The Sapthamsa chart must be consulted for this analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions regarding siblings, their welfare, and relationship dynamics will be inaccurate without consulting the Sapthamsa chart.
rules.classical_reference: Horapradeepa, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'आतृविचारस्तु सप्तांशात्'. Note: This assignment (Sapthamsa for siblings) differs from some other classical texts where Sapthamsa is for children.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing matters related to children and progeny.
check_logic.calculation: The Navamsa chart must be consulted for this analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions regarding children, their birth, welfare, and relationship dynamics will be inaccurate without consulting the Navamsa chart.
rules.classical_reference: Horapradeepa, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'संततिफलं नवांशाद्'. Note: This assignment (Navamsa for children) differs from some other classical texts where Navamsa is primarily for spouse and dharma, and Sapthamsa is for children.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing matters related to the spouse and marital life.
check_logic.calculation: The Dwadasamsa chart must be consulted for this analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions regarding marital harmony, spouse's characteristics, and marital events will be inaccurate without consulting the Dwadasamsa chart.
rules.classical_reference: Horapradeepa, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'द्वादशभात्कलतचिन्ता च'. Note: This assignment (Dwadasamsa for spouse) differs from some other classical texts where Dwadasamsa is for parents.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing matters related to death or longevity.
check_logic.calculation: The Trimsamsa chart must be consulted for this analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Assessments of longevity and circumstances surrounding death will be inaccurate without consulting the Trimsamsa chart.
rules.classical_reference: Horapradeepa, Jataka Parijata Vol 1, NOTES, Adh 1
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'सृत्योर्शानं कथितं त्रिंशांशाज्जातकशैस्तु'. Note: This assignment (Trimsamsa for death) differs from some other classical texts where Trimsamsa is for misfortunes and diseases.

rules.rule_name: Saptamsha Lord Calculation for Odd Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating Saptamsha lords for odd signs (Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius, Aquarius).
check_logic.calculation: For odd signs, the sequence of Saptamsha lords starts from the lord of the sign itself. The first Saptamsha is ruled by the sign lord, the second by the lord of the next sign, and so on, in zodiacal order.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Saptamsha chart, leading to erroneous predictions regarding children, progeny, and relationships.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 32
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting point and sequence for Saptamsha lords in odd signs.

rules.rule_name: Saptamsha Lord Calculation for Even Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating Saptamsha lords for even signs (Taurus, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, Pisces).
check_logic.calculation: For even signs, the sequence of Saptamsha lords starts from the lord of the 7th sign from the sign itself. The first Saptamsha is ruled by the lord of the 7th sign, the second by the lord of the next sign, and so on, in zodiacal order.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Saptamsha chart, leading to erroneous predictions regarding children, progeny, and relationships.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 32
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting point and sequence for Saptamsha lords in even signs.

rules.rule_name: Navamsha Lord Calculation for Fiery Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating Navamsha lords for fiery signs (Aries, Leo, Sagittarius).
check_logic.calculation: For fiery signs, the sequence of Navamsha lords starts from Aries. The first Navamsha of a fiery sign is ruled by Aries, the second by Taurus, and so on, in zodiacal order.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Navamsha chart, leading to erroneous predictions regarding marriage, spouse, and general fortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 32
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting point and sequence for Navamsha lords in fiery signs.

rules.rule_name: Navamsha Lord Calculation for Earthy Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating Navamsha lords for earthy signs (Taurus, Virgo, Capricorn).
check_logic.calculation: For earthy signs, the sequence of Navamsha lords starts from Capricorn. The first Navamsha of an earthy sign is ruled by Capricorn, the second by Aquarius, and so on, in zodiacal order.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Navamsha chart, leading to erroneous predictions regarding marriage, spouse, and general fortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 32
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for determining the starting point and sequence for Navamsha lords in earthy signs.

check_logic.condition: A planet's Navamsa position falls within the Vargottama range for its Rashi.
check_logic.calculation:
To determine Vargottama Navamsa:
- For planets in Moveable signs (Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn): The planet must be in the first Navamsa (0°00' to 3°20').
- For planets in Fixed signs (Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius): The planet must be in the middle Navamsa (13°20' to 16°40').
- For planets in Dual signs (Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces): The planet must be in the last Navamsa (26°40' to 30°00').

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet is not in Vargottama Navamsa, it does not gain the significant strength and auspiciousness associated with this status, reducing its capacity to deliver positive results and fulfill its promise.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1, Verse 35 (also references Brihat Jataka, Satya, Saravali for confirmation)
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines Vargottama Navamsa based on sign type (moveable, fixed, dual) and specifies the Navamsa range. It also states the general auspicious impact of a planet being in Vargottama.

rules.rule_name: Trimsamsa (D30) Degree Allotment in Odd Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Trimsamsa (D30) chart for a planet located in an odd sign.
check_logic.calculation:
For a planet in an odd sign, its Trimsamsa lord is determined by its degree within the sign according to the following allotments:
- Mars: 0-5 degrees
- Saturn: 5-10 degrees
- Jupiter: 10-18 degrees
- Mercury: 18-25 degrees
- Venus: 25-30 degrees

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Trimsamsa (D30) chart calculation, leading to erroneous analysis of misfortunes, character, and general well-being, as the planetary dignity and placement in D30 will be wrong.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 37
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the degree distribution for Trimsamsa (D30) lordships in odd signs. The degrees are cumulative ranges based on the allotted portions mentioned in the text.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa for a planet in a Fiery sign (Mesha, Simha, Dhanus)
check_logic.calculation: The Rashi sign is divided into nine equal parts of 3°20' each. The first Navamsa of the sign starts from Mesha (Aries). Subsequent Navamsas follow zodiacal order (Mesha, Vrishabha, Mithuna, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Navamsa calculation leads to erroneous planetary dignity and strength assessment, invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 34
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Navamsa table provided for 'Mesha Simha Dhanus' group.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa for a planet in an Earthy sign (Vrishabha, Kanya, Makara)
check_logic.calculation: The Rashi sign is divided into nine equal parts of 3°20' each. The first Navamsa of the sign starts from Makara (Capricorn). Subsequent Navamsas follow zodiacal order (Makara, Kumbha, Meena, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Navamsa calculation leads to erroneous planetary dignity and strength assessment, invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 34
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Navamsa table provided for 'Vrishabha Kanya Makara' group.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa for a planet in an Airy sign (Mithuna, Thula, Kumbha)
check_logic.calculation: The Rashi sign is divided into nine equal parts of 3°20' each. The first Navamsa of the sign starts from Thula (Libra). Subsequent Navamsas follow zodiacal order (Thula, Vrischika, Dhanus, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Navamsa calculation leads to erroneous planetary dignity and strength assessment, invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 34
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Navamsa table provided for 'Mithuna Thula Kumbha' group.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Navamsa for a planet in a Watery sign (Kataka, Vrischika, Meena)
check_logic.calculation: The Rashi sign is divided into nine equal parts of 3°20' each. The first Navamsa of the sign starts from Kataka (Cancer). Subsequent Navamsas follow zodiacal order (Kataka, Simha, Kanya, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Navamsa calculation leads to erroneous planetary dignity and strength assessment, invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 34
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Navamsa table provided for 'Kataka Vrischika Meena' group.

check_logic.condition: When determining Vargottama for a planet in a Chara (Movable) sign (Mesha, Kataka, Thula, Makara)
check_logic.calculation: A planet is Vargottama if its Navamsa sign is the same as its Rashi sign, and its longitude falls within the first Navamsa (0°00' to 3°20') of that Chara sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Vargottama assessment leads to erroneous planetary strength and promise evaluation, invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 34
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the sloka 'चेर चाद्यंशको ज्ञेयः' (For Chara, the first Navamsa is Vargottama).

check_logic.condition: When determining Vargottama for a planet in a Sthira (Fixed) sign (Vrishabha, Simha, Vrischika, Kumbha)
check_logic.calculation: A planet is Vargottama if its Navamsa sign is the same as its Rashi sign, and its longitude falls within the fifth Navamsa (13°20' to 16°40') of that Sthira sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Vargottama assessment leads to erroneous planetary strength and promise evaluation, invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 34
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the sloka 'स्थिरे मध्यनवांशकः' (For Sthira, the middle (5th) Navamsa is Vargottama).

check_logic.condition: When determining Vargottama for a planet in a Dwiswabhava (Dual) sign (Mithuna, Kanya, Dhanus, Meena)
check_logic.calculation: A planet is Vargottama if its Navamsa sign is the same as its Rashi sign, and its longitude falls within the ninth Navamsa (26°40' to 30°00') of that Dwiswabhava sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Vargottama assessment leads to erroneous planetary strength and promise evaluation, invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 34
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the sloka 'अंत्यांशको द्विस्वभादे' (For Dwiswabhava, the last (9th) Navamsa is Vargottama).

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Dasamamsa (D10) chart.
check_logic.calculation: For an odd sign, count the Dasamamsa portions from the sign itself. For an even sign, count the Dasamamsa portions from its 9th sign onwards.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dasamamsa chart construction, leading to erroneous career analysis and predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the starting point for counting Dasamamsa portions based on the nature of the sign.

check_logic.condition: When assigning lords to the Dasamamsa (D10) portions according to Parasara.
check_logic.calculation: For an odd sign, the lords of the Dasamamsas are (1) Indra, (2) Agni, (3) Yama, (4) Rakshasa, (5) Varuna, (6) Maruta, (7) Kubera, (8) Esana, (9) Padmaja, and (10) Anantha, in regular order. For an even sign, these lords are counted in the inverse order.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dasamamsa chart interpretation, leading to erroneous career analysis and predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies the deities that lord the Dasamamsa portions based on the sign's odd/even nature.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Dwadasamsa (D12) chart.
check_logic.calculation: The Dwadasamsa portions of a sign are to be reckoned from that sign itself.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dwadasamsa chart construction, leading to erroneous analysis of parents, heritage, or past karma.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the starting point for counting Dwadasamsa portions.

check_logic.condition: When assigning lords to the Dwadasamsa (D12) portions according to Parasara.
check_logic.calculation: The lords of the Dwadasamsas of each sign are (1) Ganesa, (2) Aswini Kumaras, (3) Yama, and (4) Ahi, recurring in regular order.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dwadasamsa chart interpretation, leading to erroneous analysis of parents, heritage, or past karma.
rules.extraction_notes: The text notes that 'this view is not accepted by all', but it is presented as Parasara's rule in this source.

check_logic.condition: When assigning lords to the Shodasamsa (D16) portions.
check_logic.calculation: For an odd sign, the lords of the Shodasamsas are Brahma, Vishnu, Hara, and Ravi, recurring in regular order. When the Lagna is an even sign, the lords of the Shodasamsas are to be counted in the inverse order from Bhaskara (Ravi).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Shodasamsa chart interpretation, leading to erroneous analysis of happiness, vehicles, or comforts.
rules.extraction_notes: The inverse order rule is explicitly stated for when the Lagna is an even sign, implying a specific application for the Lagna's Shodasamsa.

rules.rule_name: Shodasamsa Lordship Calculation for Odd Signs

check_logic.condition: When determining Shodasamsa (D16) sub-division lords for a planet placed in an odd sign.
check_logic.calculation: For a planet in an odd sign: The first twelve sub-divisions (1st-12th) are ruled by the lords of the twelve signs beginning from that sign (i.e., current sign, 2nd sign from current, ..., 12th sign from current). The remaining four sub-divisions (13th-16th) are ruled by the lords of the 9th, 10th, 11th, and 12th signs respectively, counted from the same current odd sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Shodasamsa chart construction, leading to erroneous analysis of happiness, unhappiness, and general fortune.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Shodasamsa calculation method for odd signs, including the detailed notes.

rules.rule_name: Shodasamsa Lordship Calculation for Even Signs

check_logic.condition: When determining Shodasamsa (D16) sub-division lords for a planet placed in an even sign.
check_logic.calculation: For a planet in an even sign: The first four sub-divisions (1st-4th) are ruled by the lords of the 12th, 11th, 10th, and 9th signs respectively, counted from the current even sign. The remaining twelve sub-divisions (5th-16th) are ruled by the lords of the twelve signs of the zodiac, counted in reverse order (i.e., Pisces, Aquarius, Capricorn, Sagittarius, Scorpio, Libra, Virgo, Leo, Cancer, Gemini, Taurus, Aries).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Shodasamsa chart construction, leading to erroneous analysis of happiness, unhappiness, and general fortune.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Shodasamsa calculation method for even signs, including the detailed notes.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity Assessment by Varga Occupation (Vargottama Classification)

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's inherent strength, dignity, or ability to deliver results.
check_logic.calculation:
For each planet, determine the number of divisional charts (vargas) where it occupies its own sign. Classify the planet's dignity based on this count according to the following definitions: 
- 2 vargas: Parijata
- 3 vargas: Uttama
- 4 vargas: Gopura
- 5 vargas: Simhasana
- 6 vargas: Paravathamsa
- 7 or 8 vargas: Devaloka
- 9 vargas: Iravatha

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary strength and dignity, leading to erroneous predictions regarding the planet's ability to deliver results and the quality of its indications.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines a hierarchical system for classifying planetary dignity based on the number of vargas a planet occupies in its own sign. Each classification represents increasing strength and is fundamental for assessing a planet's inherent capacity.

rules.rule_name: Trimsamsa (D30) Lordship for Planets in Odd Signs

check_logic.condition: WHEN calculating the Trimsamsa (D30) lord for a planet situated in an odd sign (Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius, Aquarius)
check_logic.calculation:
The Trimsamsa lord is determined by the planet's degree within the sign according to the following fixed ranges:
*   Mars: 0-5 degrees
*   Saturn: 5-10 degrees
*   Jupiter: 10-18 degrees
*   Mercury: 18-25 degrees
*   Venus: 25-30 degrees

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Trimsamsa chart construction, leading to erroneous analysis and predictions based on the D30 chart.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse ३८
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the verse describing Trimsamsa degrees for Mars, Saturn, Jupiter, Mercury, Venus in odd signs (ओजमवनेषु).

rules.rule_name: Trimsamsa (D30) Lordship for Planets in Even Signs

check_logic.condition: WHEN calculating the Trimsamsa (D30) lord for a planet situated in an even sign (Taurus, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, Pisces)
check_logic.calculation:
The Trimsamsa lord is determined by the planet's degree within the sign according to the following fixed ranges:
*   Venus: 0-5 degrees
*   Mercury: 5-12 degrees
*   Jupiter: 12-20 degrees
*   Saturn: 20-25 degrees
*   Mars: 25-30 degrees

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Trimsamsa chart construction, leading to erroneous analysis and predictions based on the D30 chart.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse ३८
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the verse describing Trimsamsa degrees for Venus, Mercury, Jupiter, Saturn, Mars in even signs (युग्मभवनेषु).

rules.rule_name: Trimsamsa (D30) Planet Placement Logic based on Odd/Even Signs

check_logic.condition: WHEN placing a planet in the Trimsamsa (D30) chart after identifying its Trimsamsa lord
check_logic.calculation:
If the natal planet is in an odd sign, it must be placed in the odd sign owned by its Trimsamsa lord in the D30 chart. If the natal planet is in an even sign, it must be placed in the even sign owned by its Trimsamsa lord in the D30 chart.
*   Example: If a planet is in an odd sign and falls in Guru's (Jupiter's) Trimsamsa, it is placed in Dhanus (Sagittarius, an odd sign owned by Jupiter). If a planet is in an even sign and falls in Guru's Trimsamsa, it is placed in Meena (Pisces, an even sign owned by Jupiter).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Trimsamsa chart construction, leading to erroneous analysis and predictions based on the D30 chart.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse ३८
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the explanatory text detailing Trimsamsa planet placement logic based on odd/even signs of the natal planet and the Trimsamsa lord.

rules.rule_name: Mandatory Trimsamsa (D30) Consideration for Female Horoscopes

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the horoscope of a female native
check_logic.calculation: The Trimsamsa (D30) chart must be constructed and its indications considered for a comprehensive and accurate analysis of the native's character, fortune, and marital happiness.

rules.impact_if_violated: Analysis of the female native's horoscope will be incomplete or less accurate, particularly concerning aspects related to character, fortune, and marital happiness.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse ३८
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the explicit statement 'Trimsamsa (त्रिशांश) divisions are greatly useful in female horoscopes'.

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's inherent strength and dignity across divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: A planet is classified as Trivargaikyam if it occupies the same sign in 3 divisional charts (Vargas).

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet does not achieve Trivargaikyam, it indicates that its inherent dignity and ability to deliver consistent results are not at this particular elevated level, potentially being at a lower classification or not classified by this system.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 45
rules.extraction_notes: Describes a classification of planetary strength based on occupying the same sign in 3 divisional charts (Vargas). This is considered 'excellent'.

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's inherent strength and dignity across divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: A planet is classified as Gopuram if it occupies the same sign in 4 divisional charts (Vargas).

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet does not achieve Gopuram, it indicates that its inherent dignity and ability to deliver consistent results are not at this particular elevated level, potentially being at a lower classification or not classified by this system.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 45
rules.extraction_notes: Describes a classification of planetary strength based on occupying the same sign in 4 divisional charts (Vargas).

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's inherent strength and dignity across divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: A planet is classified as Simhasanam if it occupies the same sign in 5 divisional charts (Vargas).

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet does not achieve Simhasanam, it indicates that its inherent dignity and ability to deliver consistent results are not at this particular elevated level, potentially being at a lower classification or not classified by this system.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 45
rules.extraction_notes: Describes a classification of planetary strength based on occupying the same sign in 5 divisional charts (Vargas).

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength Assessment via Saptha Varga

check_logic.condition: When determining the inherent strength and efficacy of a planet for any astrological analysis.
check_logic.calculation: The Saptha Varga (Lagna, Hora, Drekkana, Navamsa, Dwadasamsa, Thrimsamsa, and Sapthamamsa) must be calculated and considered as a source of strength for the planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete or inaccurate assessment of planetary inherent strength, leading to potentially flawed predictions regarding the planet's capacity to deliver results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 48
rules.extraction_notes: This rule emphasizes the necessity of considering Saptha Varga for planetary strength, as it is explicitly stated as 'a source of strength to the planets in all horoscopes'.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing any horoscope
check_logic.calculation: The 'Vilagne' (बिलप्ने) refers to the rising sign (ascendant) at the time of birth.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the Lagna will render the entire chart analysis fundamentally flawed and unreliable.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly states 'Vilagne (विलप्ने) can only mean the 'rising sign'.

check_logic.condition: When interpreting any sign, especially the Lagna
check_logic.calculation: Signs possess inherent qualities such as 'positive or negative' and 'male or female'. These qualities must be considered in analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Ignoring the inherent qualities of signs will lead to incomplete or inaccurate interpretations of planetary placements and house significations.
rules.extraction_notes: The verse considers the possibility of a 'rising sign' being 'positive, or negative, or male or female'.

check_logic.condition: When detailed analysis of misfortunes, character, or specific life areas (e.g., health, longevity, adverse events) is required
check_logic.calculation: Trimsamsa (त्रिशांशकाः) is a valid and essential divisional chart used in astrological analysis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Omitting Trimsamsa analysis when relevant will result in a superficial understanding of a native's challenges and specific karmic patterns.
rules.extraction_notes: The verse explicitly mentions 'त्रिशांशकाः' (Trimsamsa).

check_logic.condition: When calculating or interpreting the Trimsamsa chart
check_logic.calculation: The Trimsamsa divisions are ruled by specific planets: Venus (सित), Mercury (विदार्य), Saturn (शनि), Mars (क्षमाजा), and Jupiter (जा).

rules.impact_if_violated: Using incorrect planetary lords for Trimsamsa divisions will lead to entirely erroneous Trimsamsa chart calculations and interpretations.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly lists 'त्रिशांशकाः सितविदार्यशनिक्षमाजाः'.

rules.rule_name: Trimsamsa Rulership Variation by Odd/Even Signs

check_logic.condition: When calculating Trimsamsa divisions for a planet in any sign
check_logic.calculation: The allocation of Trimsamsa rulerships (planets and their respective degrees within a sign) varies depending on whether the sign is odd (ओजे) or even (युग्मे).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to differentiate Trimsamsa rulerships based on odd or even signs will result in incorrect divisional chart calculations and subsequent misinterpretations.
rules.extraction_notes: The verse uses 'ओजे' (odd signs) and 'युग्मे' (even signs) to describe different sets of divisions. The specific degree allocations mentioned in the verse are ambiguous and do not consistently sum to 30 degrees, thus only the principle of variation is extracted as a non-negotiable rule.

rules.rule_name: Assessment of Planetary Dignity for Vaiseshikamsas (Uttama)

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's potential to confer 'immense good' through Vaiseshikamsas (e.g., Uttama).
check_logic.calculation: For each planet, check if it occupies its Moolatrikona sign, or its Swakshetra (own sign), or its Uccha (exaltation) sign, or its Vargottama position (same sign in Rasi and Navamsa). The presence and combination of these specific dignities, as assessed across the Dasa Vargas, indicate the formation of Vaiseshikamsas.

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet does not exhibit these specific dignities (Moolatrikona, Swakshetra, Uccha, Vargottama) across the Dasa Vargas, it will not form Vaiseshikamsas (like Uttama) and thus will not confer the 'immense good' associated with these states.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the foundational dignities (Moolatrikona, Swakshetra, Uccha, Vargottama) that, when combined and assessed across the Dasa Vargas, lead to the formation of Vaiseshikamsas, commencing with Uttama. The text emphasizes the 'combination' of these characteristics 'arising from the ten vargas'.

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's dignity and strength across multiple divisional charts (Vargas).
check_logic.calculation: A planet is classified as Parijata if it occupies its own sign, exaltation sign, or a friendly sign in exactly 2 divisional charts (Vargas) out of a defined set (e.g., Sapta Varga or Dasavarga).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of a planet's inherent dignity and potential, leading to erroneous promise assessment.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the Parijata Vaiseshikamsa based on the number of Vargas a planet occupies in its own/exaltation/friendly signs.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent qualities (soul, mind, strength, speech, knowledge, happiness, desire, misery) of a native.
check_logic.calculation: To ascertain the inherent qualities of a person, the astrologer MUST analyze the strength, dignity, and placement of the respective karaka planet (Sun for soul, Moon for mind, Mars for strength, Mercury for speech, Jupiter for knowledge/happiness, Venus for desire, Saturn for misery).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the native's fundamental inherent qualities.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule establishes the fundamental karakatwas of planets for the inherent qualities of a person, linking them to the Kalapurusha concept.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the physical body parts of the Kalapurusha or the native.
check_logic.calculation: For Kalapurusha, body parts MUST be counted from Mesha (Aries) as the head. For the native, body parts MUST be counted from the Lagna (Ascendant) as the head.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body parts associated with specific signs/houses, leading to erroneous health or physical characteristic predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Adhyaya II, Sloka 1 Notes (referencing Adhyaya I, Sloka 8)
rules.extraction_notes: This rule clarifies the starting point for mapping body parts to signs (for Kalapurusha) and houses (for the native's horoscope).

check_logic.condition: To assess Jupiter's highest dignity (Devalokamsa) based on varga positions.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Jupiter occupies its Moolatrikona, Swadrekkana, Swasapthamsa, Swanavamsa, Swadasamamsa, Swadwadasamsa, AND Swakalamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Jupiter does not attain Devalokamsa, indicating lesser strength or auspiciousness from this specific dignity.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from an example illustrating a specific planetary dignity calculation based on multiple varga occupations.

check_logic.condition: To assess Mars's high dignity (Uttamamsa) based on varga positions.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Mars occupies its own Drekkana, a Vargottama Navamsa, AND its own Thrimsamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Mars does not attain Uttamamsa, indicating lesser strength or auspiciousness from this specific dignity.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from an example illustrating a specific planetary dignity calculation based on multiple varga occupations.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Lagna or First House in a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: The Lagna signifies: Kalpa, Udaya (rising), Adya (first), Thanu (body), Janma (birth), Vilagna, and Hora.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of the native's self, physical body, general disposition, and overall life trajectory.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the primary significations and alternative names for the First House/Lagna.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Second House in a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: The Second House signifies: Vak (speech), Artha (wealth), Bhukthi (food), Nayana (eye), Sva (property), and Kutumba (family).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of matters related to wealth, family, speech, sustenance, and movable property.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the primary significations and alternative names for the Second House.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Third House in a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: The Third House signifies: Duschikya, Vikrama (prowess), Sahodara (brother), Virya (heroism), Dhairya (firmness), and Karna (ear).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of matters related to siblings, courage, short travels, communication, and self-effort.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the primary significations and alternative names for the Third House.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Fourth House in a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: The Fourth House signifies: Pathala, Vriddhi, Hibuka, Kshiti (land), Matri (mother), Vidya (learning), Yana (vehicle), Ambu (water), Geha (house), Sukha (happiness), Bandhu (relations), and Chathushtaya.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of matters related to mother, home, happiness, education, vehicles, and immovable property.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the primary significations and alternative names for the Fourth House.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Fifth House in a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: The Fifth House signifies: Dhi (intelligence), Deva (deity), Raja (king), Pitrunandana (father son), and Panchaka.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of matters related to children, intelligence, spiritual merit, past life karma, and speculation.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the primary significations and alternative names for the Fifth House.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Sixth House in a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: The Sixth House signifies: Roga (disease), Amsa (partition), Sasthra (weapon), Bhaya (danger), Shashta, Ripu (enemy), and Kshata (wounds).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of matters related to health, enemies, debt, service, challenges, and litigation.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the primary significations and alternative names for the Sixth House.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing matters related to the body, health, or general self.
check_logic.calculation: Ascertain from the Lagna (1st house).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of physical well-being and general disposition.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This defines the primary significations of the Lagna (1st house).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of financial status and family relations.

rules.extraction_notes: This defines the primary significations of the 2nd house.

rules.extraction_notes: This defines the primary significations of the 3rd house.

check_logic.condition: When classifying houses based on their inherent nature of growth or increase.
check_logic.calculation: Houses 3, 6, 10, 11 are Upachaya houses (signs of increase or growth).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the inherent nature of these houses, leading to incorrect predictions regarding growth and improvement over time.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Upachaya houses, which are generally considered beneficial for growth and improvement over time.

check_logic.condition: When classifying houses based on their inherent nature of suffering or difficulty.
check_logic.calculation: Houses 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 12 are Apachaya houses (houses of suffering).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the inherent nature of these houses, leading to incorrect predictions regarding areas of life that may experience challenges or suffering.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Apachaya houses, referred to as 'houses of suffering' in the text, indicating areas of life prone to difficulties.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the auspiciousness of a house's effects.
check_logic.calculation: A house produces auspicious effects IF it is: 1. Occupied by its own lord, OR 2. Aspected by its own lord, OR 3. Occupied by Budha (Mercury), Sukra (Venus), or Guru (Jupiter), OR 4. Aspected by Budha (Mercury), Sukra (Venus), or Guru (Jupiter). AND 5. It is *not* occupied by any other planet (especially malefics or inimical planets). 6. It is *not* aspected by any other planet (especially malefics or inimical planets).

rules.impact_if_violated: The house will not produce auspicious effects, or its effects will be mixed/inauspicious, leading to an incorrect assessment of the potential for positive outcomes in that area of life.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies conditions for a house to yield auspicious results, emphasizing the role of its lord and natural benefics, while excluding other planets (implied to be malefics or inimical ones).

rules.rule_name: Upachaya House Cancellation by Malefic Aspect (Garga's View)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the Upachaya nature of houses 3, 6, 10, 11.
check_logic.calculation: If an Upachaya house (3rd, 6th, 10th, or 11th) is aspected by a malefic or inimical planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The house ceases to be considered an Upachaya house, meaning its inherent nature of growth and increase is nullified or severely reduced, leading to an incorrect assessment of its potential for improvement.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This is a specific condition, attributed to Garga, that negates the Upachaya status of houses 3, 6, 10, 11 if malefics or inimical planets aspect them.

rules.rule_name: Lagna's strength for a satisfactory horoscope

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall quality and satisfactory nature of a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Lagna (e.g., Lagna Lord's strength, benefic aspects on Lagna, lack of malefic aspects, placement of Lagna Lord). The Lagna must be strong.

rules.impact_if_violated: The horoscope is not considered satisfactory at a fundamental level.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the phrase 'not to say of the Lagna' implying Lagna's strength is a fundamental prerequisite for a satisfactory horoscope.

rules.rule_name: Moon's strength for a satisfactory horoscope

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall quality and satisfactory nature of a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Moon (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, Paksha Bala, placement). The Moon must be strong.

rules.impact_if_violated: The horoscope is not considered satisfactory, and its fundamental strength is compromised, especially regarding mental well-being and general prosperity.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the statement 'In a horoscope, the Sun and the Moon must...be strong; otherwise it is not a satisfactory one. Of these, the Moon is the most important.'

rules.rule_name: Sun's strength for a satisfactory horoscope

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall quality and satisfactory nature of a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Sun (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, placement). The Sun must be strong.

rules.impact_if_violated: The horoscope is not considered satisfactory, impacting vitality, authority, and overall well-being.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the statement 'In a horoscope, the Sun and the Moon must...be strong; otherwise it is not a satisfactory one.'

rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the list of planetary portfolios/roles. Sun is the King.
