rules.extraction_notes: Specific yoga for illegitimate offspring. 'नीचस्थिताश्चन्द्रदिवाकरेज्याः कुर्वन्ध्यमी जन्मनि जारजातम्' means Moon, Sun, Jupiter in debilitation cause illegitimate birth.

rules.rule_name: Illegitimacy due to Jupiter in 9th, Moon in 2nd, and Moon in Sun's orb

check_logic.condition: When assessing the legitimacy of offspring.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Jupiter is in the 9th house, Moon is in the 2nd house, AND Moon is in conjunction with the Sun (or within Sun's orb).

rules.impact_if_violated: Offspring is declared illegitimate.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 58
rules.extraction_notes: Specific yoga for illegitimate offspring. 'नवमस्यो गुरुयंत्र धने चन्द्रोऽर्कमण्डले । अन्यजातः स विशेयो योगेऽस्मिन्यसते भवम् ॥' - 'अर्कमण्डले' interpreted as Moon in Sun's orb/conjunction.

rules.rule_name: Illegitimacy due to Moon, Mars, Sun in one house and Jupiter in 5th

check_logic.condition: When assessing the legitimacy of offspring.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Moon, Mars, AND Sun are in the same house, AND Jupiter is in the 5th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Offspring is declared illegitimate.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 58
rules.extraction_notes: Specific yoga for illegitimate offspring. 'चन्द्रारभानवः यहे गुरुः पश्चमगो यदि । योगेऽमिश्रात्र सन्देहश्राम्यजतः स उच्यते ॥'

rules.rule_name: Illegitimacy due to birth on Tuesday with 4th or 14th Tithi

check_logic.condition: When assessing the legitimacy of offspring.
check_logic.calculation: Check if birth occurs on a Tuesday (Mangalvar) AND the Tithi is 4th (Chaturthi) OR 14th (Chaturdashi).

rules.impact_if_violated: Offspring is declared illegitimate.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 58
rules.extraction_notes: Specific yoga for illegitimate offspring. 'भौमदिने चतुर्दशी स्थाञ्जातजारस्य च जन्मकाले ॥'

rules.rule_name: Illegitimacy due to birth at the end of Tithi, Karana, or Vara

check_logic.condition: When assessing the legitimacy of offspring.
check_logic.calculation: Check if birth occurs at the very end of a Tithi, Karana, OR Vara (day).

rules.impact_if_violated: Offspring is declared illegitimate.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 58
rules.extraction_notes: Specific yoga for illegitimate offspring. 'विनरन्ते च तिथिप्रान्ते कमप्रान्ते प्रसूतिषु । वारस्यान्ते च यो जातः सोऽभ्यगातः प्रकीर्त्तितः ॥'

rules.rule_name: Illegitimacy due to Bhadra Tithi with Sun-Saturn conjunction

check_logic.condition: When assessing the legitimacy of offspring.
check_logic.calculation: Check if birth occurs during a Bhadra Tithi (Dwitheeya, Dwadasi, Sapthami) AND Sun and Saturn are in conjunction.

rules.impact_if_violated: Offspring is declared illegitimate.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 58
rules.extraction_notes: Specific yoga for illegitimate offspring. 'भन्नपादर्शसंयोगाद्वितीया द्वादशी यदि । सप्तमी चार्कमन्दारे जारजो जायते ध्रुवम् ॥' - 'अर्कमन्दारे' means Sun and Saturn.

check_logic.condition: When determining the direction of a lying-in chamber for childbirth.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet with the highest Shadbala (or other recognized strength assessment method) among planets located in Kendras (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th houses). Identify the sign that is owned by this strongest planet. This sign will then be used to determine the direction of the lying-in chamber.

rules.impact_if_violated: The foundational sign for determining the lying-in chamber's direction will be incorrectly identified, leading to an erroneous prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 73
rules.extraction_notes: This rule establishes the primary method for identifying the astrological factor (sign) that dictates the direction of the lying-in chamber.

check_logic.condition: If the sign identified as per the 'Lying-in Chamber Direction: Determination Method' rule is Mesha (Aries), Kataka (Cancer), Tula (Libra), Vrischika (Scorpio), or Kumbha (Aquarius).
check_logic.calculation: Map the identified sign to the corresponding direction.

rules.impact_if_violated: The direction of the lying-in chamber will be incorrectly determined, leading to an erroneous prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 73
rules.extraction_notes: This rule maps specific signs to the Eastern direction for the lying-in chamber.

check_logic.condition: If the sign identified as per the 'Lying-in Chamber Direction: Determination Method' rule is owned by Jupiter (Dhanu/Sagittarius, Meena/Pisces) or Mercury (Mithuna/Gemini, Kanya/Virgo).
check_logic.calculation: Map the identified sign (Dhanu, Meena, Mithuna, or Kanya) to the corresponding direction.

rules.impact_if_violated: The direction of the lying-in chamber will be incorrectly determined, leading to an erroneous prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 73
rules.extraction_notes: This rule maps signs owned by Jupiter or Mercury to the Northern direction for the lying-in chamber. Explicitly, these signs are Sagittarius, Pisces, Gemini, and Virgo.

check_logic.condition: If the sign identified as per the 'Lying-in Chamber Direction: Determination Method' rule is Vrishabha (Taurus).
check_logic.calculation: Map the identified sign to the corresponding direction.

rules.impact_if_violated: The direction of the lying-in chamber will be incorrectly determined, leading to an erroneous prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 73
rules.extraction_notes: This rule maps the sign Taurus to the Western direction for the lying-in chamber.

check_logic.condition: If the sign identified as per the 'Lying-in Chamber Direction: Determination Method' rule is Makara (Capricorn) or Simha (Leo).
check_logic.calculation: Map the identified sign to the corresponding direction.

rules.impact_if_violated: The direction of the lying-in chamber will be incorrectly determined, leading to an erroneous prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 73
rules.extraction_notes: This rule maps specific signs to the Southern direction for the lying-in chamber.

check_logic.condition: Before any astrological analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 12 houses (Bhavas) based on the Lagna and chosen house system (e.g., Placidus, Sripati, Equal).

rules.impact_if_violated: All subsequent analysis and predictions will be fundamentally incorrect due to misidentification of house significations.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text refers to '4th Bhava from the Lagna', implicitly validating the necessity of identifying houses as a fundamental step in astrological analysis.

check_logic.condition: When assessing planetary dignity and strength.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if a planet is placed in its sign of debilitation (Neecha Rashi) according to classical definitions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary strength, dignity, and potential for Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga; leading to erroneous predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text refers to 'depression signs' (debilitation), implying that the identification of a planet's debilitation state is a fundamental and non-negotiable step in astrological analysis.

check_logic.condition: When assessing planetary influences and relationships.
check_logic.calculation: Identify all planetary aspects (e.g., full, three-quarter, half, quarter aspects) based on classical rules (e.g., BPHS aspects for Jupiter, Saturn, Mars).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary influences, yogas, and overall chart dynamics, leading to significant predictive errors.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text refers to 'aspected by Saturn', implicitly validating the necessity of identifying planetary aspects as a fundamental step in astrological analysis.

check_logic.condition: When assessing planetary influences and relationships.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets in conjunction based on classical orb definitions (e.g., within 5-10 degrees).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary influences, yogas, and overall chart dynamics, leading to significant predictive errors.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text refers to 'in conjunction with Saturn', implicitly validating the necessity of identifying planetary conjunctions as a fundamental step in astrological analysis.

check_logic.condition: When assessing planetary strength, dignity, and specific life areas like marriage and general fortune.
check_logic.calculation: Construct and analyze the Navamsa (D9) chart to determine planetary placements, dignities, and aspects within it.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete or inaccurate assessment of planetary strength, dignity, and specific life areas, especially marriage and general fortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text refers to 'Moon in a Navamsa owned by Saturn', which implicitly validates the necessity of analyzing the Navamsa (D9) chart as a fundamental step in astrological analysis.

rules.rule_name: Sign Element Classification (Watery, Fiery, Earthy, Airy)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the fundamental nature and characteristics of signs and planets placed within them.
check_logic.calculation: Classify each sign according to its elemental nature (Watery, Fiery, Earthy, Airy) based on classical definitions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of sign characteristics and their influence on planets and houses, leading to erroneous predictions regarding temperament, environment, and events.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text refers to 'occupies a watery sign', implicitly validating the necessity of classifying signs by their elemental nature as a fundamental step in astrological analysis.

rules.rule_name: Sign Classification for Birth Posture (Seershodaya, Prushtodaya, Ubhayodaya)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing birth-related charts or specific characteristics of signs.
check_logic.calculation: Classify each sign as Seershodaya (head-rising), Prushtodaya (back-rising), or Ubhayodaya (both-rising) based on classical definitions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect predictions regarding birth posture and other sign-specific interpretations related to the 'rising' nature of the sign.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text explicitly mentions and uses the classifications of signs into Seershodaya, Prushtodaya, and Ubhayodaya, indicating that these classifications are fundamental and must be known/applied.

rules.rule_name: Lifespan prediction limitation for children under 12 years

check_logic.condition: WHEN predicting the lifespan or analyzing the health/longevity of a native.
check_logic.calculation: Check the age of the native. If the native's age is less than 12 years.

rules.impact_if_violated: It is difficult or impossible to determine the lifespan of a child up to 12 years of age with certainty. Predictions regarding longevity for this age group are highly unreliable.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule highlights a fundamental limitation in predictive astrology for very young children, attributing early death to parental karma and specific childhood afflictions (Bālagrahas) which are not detailed in this specific verse.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing physical characteristics or health of body parts.
check_logic.calculation: Lagna represents the head; 2nd house, the face; 3rd, the neck; and so on for other houses.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body parts associated with houses, leading to erroneous physical predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Fundamental mapping for physical analysis. The 'and so on' implies a standard sequence for all 12 houses.

rules.rule_name: Sign Length Classification for Physical Stature

check_logic.condition: When assessing the length or proportion of body parts based on the Rasi governing them.
check_logic.calculation: Classify Rasis as 'short', 'long', or 'even' based on classical texts (e.g., Jatakaparijata 1-13). The text provides an example sequence: 'Even Short Short Even Short Even Even Long Even Long Long Long' which needs to be mapped to specific signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the length or proportion of body parts, leading to erroneous physical predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: The text provides the categories but not the explicit sign mapping. The rule is that such a classification *must* be used.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Influence on Body Part Stature/Length

check_logic.condition: When assessing the length or proportion of body parts, considering planets occupying the relevant Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: Assign influence to planets: Moon and Saturn indicate long form/stature; Mars indicates short stature; Sun, Mercury, and Venus indicate medium stature.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary influence on body part length, leading to erroneous physical predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Directly stated planetary influences on stature.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing specific body parts within a house, especially for detailed physical characteristics.
check_logic.calculation: The first decanate (Drekkana) of a house indicates the head, eyes, ears, nostrils, cheeks, jaws, and mouth. The second decanate corresponds to the neck. (The third decanate is implied for other parts).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of specific body parts associated with Drekkanas, leading to erroneous detailed physical predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Provides a more granular mapping of body parts to Drekkanas within each house.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Navamsha Lord's Influence on Body and Complexion

check_logic.condition: When assessing the native's physical form (Murti) and complexion (Varna).
check_logic.calculation: The physical form (Murti) of the native should be predicted based on the Lagna Navamsha lord, especially if it is strong (balasamyutadgrahavapi). The complexion (Varna) should be predicted based on the lord of the Navamsha occupied by the Moon.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the native's physical appearance and complexion.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is derived from the Sanskrit verses quoted in the text, which emphasize the role of the Navamsha lord of Lagna and Moon's Navamsha lord for physical attributes.

check_logic.condition: When attempting to determine the native's lifespan (Ayus).
check_logic.calculation: Check the native's current age.

rules.impact_if_violated: Inaccurate or unreliable determination of lifespan, leading to potentially distressing and incorrect predictions for children.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from a direct statement indicating a fundamental limitation on astrological analysis for a specific age group.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential life span of a native.
check_logic.calculation: Classify the native's potential life span into one of the following categories: Balāriṣṭa (up to 8 years), Yogāriṣṭa (up to 20 years), Alpa āyu (up to 32 years), Madhya āyu (up to 70 years), or Pūrṇa āyu (up to 100 years).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the native's potential life span, leading to erroneous longevity predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 3
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the classical categories for life span assessment based on age ranges.

check_logic.condition: When attempting to determine the period of life (longevity) for a native.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the native's current age is within the first twelve years.

rules.impact_if_violated: Attempting to predict longevity for an age range where classical texts state it is not reliably possible, leading to unreliable or invalid predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 1
rules.extraction_notes: This rule establishes a fundamental limitation on the scope of longevity prediction for very young children.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength or benefic/malefic nature of Venus.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the angular distance between Venus and the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet (Venus) loses its natural beneficence and becomes 'Asubha' (inauspicious/malefic) or significantly weakened.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule explicitly states Venus becomes Asubha if within 5 degrees of Sun.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall strength and manifestation potential of a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the dignity (e.g., exaltation, own sign, friendly, neutral, inimical, debilitation) of the planet in the Navamsa (D9) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give auspicious results is significantly reduced, or it may give inauspicious results ('Asubha'). Its promise in Rashi may not fully manifest.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly states Venus becomes Asubha if weak in Navamsa. 'Satru navamsa' is given as an example of weakness.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and nature of a planet in the Rashi (D1) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in a sign ruled by a planet with whom it has an inimical relationship (as per classical planetary friendship tables).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give auspicious results is reduced, or it may give inauspicious results ('Asubha'). Its natural significations may suffer.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly states Venus becomes Asubha if in an inimical house. This is a fundamental dignity rule.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and nature of a planet in the Rashi (D1) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its own sign (Swagriha).

rules.impact_if_violated: If not in Swagriha, the planet lacks a significant source of inherent strength and comfort, potentially reducing its ability to give strong, positive results.
rules.extraction_notes: Mentioned as a benchmark for strength: 'less powerful than Swagriha'.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and nature of a planet in the Rashi (D1) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its sign of exaltation (Uchcha).

rules.impact_if_violated: If not in Uchcha, the planet lacks a significant source of extreme inherent strength and auspiciousness, potentially reducing its ability to give exceptionally strong, positive results.
rules.extraction_notes: Mentioned as a benchmark for strength: 'less powerful than Uchcha'.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing health, adversaries, or general well-being.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if a planet classified as a natural malefic (or functional malefic, though not specified here) is placed in the 6th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates potential for ulcers, wounds, or health issues related to the body part signified by the Rasi.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from an alternative reading of the sloka. Assumes 'malefic planet' refers to natural malefics unless otherwise specified.

rules.rule_name: House Signification: Mother's Head/Face and Lying Direction

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the head, face, or lying direction of a mother (lying-in-woman) based on her birth chart or a Prasna chart.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 1st (Lagna) and 2nd houses from the Lagna. These houses indicate the head and face of the mother and her lying direction.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the mother's head/face or lying direction, leading to erroneous health or situational analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the signification of Lagna and 2nd bhava for the head and face of a lying-in-woman/mother, and her lying direction.

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the fore-legs of a couch, especially in the context of a lying-in-woman.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 3rd and 12th bhavas from the Lagna. These houses represent the fore-legs of the couch, with the 3rd bhava indicating the right fore-leg.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of couch fore-legs, leading to erroneous analysis related to the couch.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the signification of 3rd and 12th bhavas for the fore-legs of a couch.

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the right side of a couch, especially in the context of a lying-in-woman.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 4th and 5th bhavas from the Lagna. These houses represent the right side of the couch.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the couch's right side, leading to erroneous analysis related to the couch.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the signification of 4th and 5th bhavas for the right side of a couch.

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the hind legs of a couch, especially in the context of a lying-in-woman.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 6th and 9th bhavas from the Lagna. These houses represent the hind legs of the couch, with the 6th bhava indicating the right hind leg.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of couch hind legs, leading to erroneous analysis related to the couch.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the signification of 6th and 9th bhavas for the hind legs of a couch.

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the legs of a lying-in-woman based on her birth chart or a Prasna chart.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 7th and 8th bhavas from the Lagna. These houses represent the legs of the lying-in-woman.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the mother's legs, leading to erroneous health or situational analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the signification of 7th and 8th bhavas for the legs of a lying-in-woman.

rules.rule_name: House Signification: Left Side of Body (General)

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the left side of a person's body based on their birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 10th and 11th houses from the Lagna. These houses represent the left side of the body.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the left side of the body, leading to erroneous health analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the signification of 10th and 11th bhavas for the left side of the body.

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the four corners of a couch, especially in the context of a lying-in-woman.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 3rd, 6th, 9th, and 12th houses from the Lagna. These houses indicate the four corners of the couch.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of couch corners, leading to erroneous analysis related to the couch.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule reiterates and consolidates the signification of 3rd, 6th, 9th, and 12th houses for the four corners of a couch.

rules.rule_name: Body Part Mapping: Visible/Invisible Zodiac for Mother's Body Sides

check_logic.condition: WHEN analyzing the physical body parts (left/right sides) of a mother based on her birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the visible half of the zodiac (above the horizon) and the invisible half (below the horizon) at the time of birth. The visible half corresponds to the left part of the mother's body. The invisible half corresponds to the right part of the mother's body.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body sides for astrological analysis, leading to erroneous health predictions or diagnoses for the mother.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines a specific mapping for the left and right sides of a mother's body based on the visible/invisible halves of the zodiac at birth, as referenced from Saravali. This is a foundational mapping rule for specific health-related queries concerning a mother.

rules.rule_name: Malefic in 6th house for brother's death

check_logic.condition: A malignant planet is in the 6th Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Identify malefic planets (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu, Sun, Waning Moon). Check if any of these planets are placed in the 6th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: May occasion a brother's death.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Direct statement from the text, indicating a possibility ('may occasion').

check_logic.condition: The rising sign (Lagna) is aspected by malefic planets AND the Moon is aspected by malefic planets AND the rising sign (Lagna) is NOT aspected by or connected with benefic planets AND the Moon is NOT aspected by or connected with benefic planets AND Jupiter is NOT in a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th).
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify malefic planets (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu, Sun, Waning Moon). 2. Identify benefic planets (e.g., Jupiter, Venus, Mercury if unafflicted, Waxing Moon). 3. Check if Lagna is aspected by any malefic. 4. Check if Moon is aspected by any malefic. 5. Check if Lagna is aspected or conjoined by any benefic. 6. Check if Moon is aspected or conjoined by any benefic. 7. Check if Jupiter is placed in the 1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th house from Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: The mother of the newborn infant will die.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Multiple conditions must be met simultaneously for the outcome, indicating certainty ('will die').

rules.rule_name: Pregnancy Death Yoga: Lagna & Moon Hemmed by Malefics without Benefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: If Lagna and Moon are both hemmed between malefic planets (malefics in the 2nd and 12th houses from them respectively), AND neither Lagna nor Moon are aspected by any benefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify Lagna and Moon's positions. 2. Check for malefic planets in the 2nd and 12th houses from Lagna. 3. Check for malefic planets in the 2nd and 12th houses from Moon. 4. Check for benefic aspects on Lagna. 5. Check for benefic aspects on Moon. (Malefics: Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, Waning Moon. Benefics: Jupiter, Venus, Mercury (unafflicted), Waxing Moon).

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates a high risk of death for the pregnant woman.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Lagna or Moon is aspected by a benefic planet.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The yoga for death is cancelled or significantly mitigated.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 11 (Varaha & Gunakara)
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is stated by both Varaha and Gunakara, emphasizing its importance. The condition 'devoid of benefic aspect' is integral to the yoga.

rules.rule_name: Pregnancy Death Yoga: Malefics in 6, 8, 12 & Venus/Jupiter Hemmed by Malefics without Benefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: If malefic planets occupy the 6th, 8th, and 12th houses, AND these malefics are devoid of benefic aspect, AND either Venus or Jupiter is hemmed between malefic planets.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify malefic planets (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, Waning Moon). 2. Check if malefics are present in the 6th, 8th, and 12th houses. 3. Check if these malefics are aspected by any benefic planet. 4. Check if Venus is hemmed between malefics. 5. Check if Jupiter is hemmed between malefics. (Benefics: Jupiter, Venus, Mercury (unafflicted), Waxing Moon).

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates immediate death for the pregnant woman along with her child.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If the malefics in the 6th, 8th, or 12th houses are aspected by a benefic planet.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The yoga for death is cancelled or significantly mitigated.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 11
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies the death of both mother and child. The condition 'devoid of benefic aspect' is integral to the yoga.

check_logic.condition: Moon is placed in a Gandanta Nakshatra.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the specific degrees of Gandanta Nakshatras (e.g., last 3°20' of Revati, Ashlesha, Jyeshtha; first 3°20' of Ashwini, Magha, Moola). Check if Moon's longitude falls within these ranges.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates inherent weakness and vulnerability, especially for a child's longevity and health. The severity increases significantly with malefic affliction.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'गण्डान्ततारासहिते मृगाङ्के'. This is the base condition for further afflictions.

rules.rule_name: Moon in Gandanta Nakshatra afflicted by malefics

check_logic.condition: Moon is in a Gandanta Nakshatra AND aspected by malefics OR conjoined with malefics.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Check if Moon is in Gandanta Nakshatra. 2. Identify natural malefics (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury with malefics). 3. Check if any natural malefic aspects the Moon (e.g., full house aspects, special aspects). 4. Check if any natural malefic is conjoined with the Moon (within orb). 5. If both 1 and (3 or 4) are true, the condition is met.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates severe affliction, potential for immediate early death or significant health issues, especially for a child ('बालो लयं याति').
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'पहाटरिःफगश्चन्द्रः क्ररैश्व सह वीक्षितः' (assuming पहाटरिःफग refers to Gandanta) and 'गण्डान्ततारासहिते मृगाङ्के पापेक्षिते पापसमन्विते वा । बालो लयं याति'.

rules.rule_name: Moon in Gandanta Nakshatra aspected by benefics

check_logic.condition: Moon is in a Gandanta Nakshatra AND aspected by benefics.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Check if Moon is in Gandanta Nakshatra. 2. Identify natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury with benefics). 3. Check if any natural benefic aspects the Moon. 4. If both 1 and 3 are true, the condition is met.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates a severe affliction, potentially leading to death, though possibly delayed (e.g., 'death in 8 years' as per the first line's interpretation). Still a critical factor for longevity.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'त्वष्टर्वषैः शुभेक्षितः' (aspected by benefics, death in 8 years) in the context of 'पहाटरिःफगश्चन्द्रः' (Moon in Gandanta).

rules.rule_name: Moon in Mrityubhaga afflicted by malefics

check_logic.condition: Moon is in a Mrityubhaga (death-inflicting degree) AND aspected by malefics.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the specific Mrityubhaga degrees for the Moon in each sign. 2. Check if Moon's longitude falls within its Mrityubhaga degree. 3. Identify natural malefics. 4. Check if any natural malefic aspects the Moon. 5. If both 2 and 4 are true, the condition is met.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates severe affliction, potential for early death or significant health issues, especially for a child ('बालो लयं याति').
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'समृत्युभागे चन्द्रे तथा पापनिरीक्षिते वा । बालो लयं याति'.

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the influence or potential of any planet in a chart.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the planet's strength using classical methods (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, etc.). The text implies a need to determine if a planet possesses 'abundant strength'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of a planet's ability to deliver its results, leading to flawed predictions regarding its benefic or malefic potential.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 14
rules.extraction_notes: The verse mentions 'Sun possessing abundant strength' as a condition for a specific yoga, implying that the assessment of planetary strength is a fundamental and non-negotiable validation rule in astrological analysis.

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the influence or potential of the Moon in a chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Moon is waxing (Shukla Paksha) or waning (Krishna Paksha). The text specifically refers to the Moon being 'on the wane'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the Moon's inherent benefic or malefic nature, which significantly impacts its role in the chart and its effects on various aspects of life.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 14
rules.extraction_notes: The verse mentions 'Moon is either on the wane' as a condition for a specific yoga, indicating that the assessment of the Moon's paksha is a fundamental and non-negotiable validation rule for understanding its nature.

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the influence of any planet, particularly the Moon, in a chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is in conjunction with any malefic planet (e.g., Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, or a waning Moon). The text specifically mentions 'Moon ... in conjunction with a malefic planet'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misjudgment of a planet's affliction or enhancement, leading to an inaccurate understanding of its capacity to produce good or bad results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 14
rules.extraction_notes: The verse mentions 'Moon ... in conjunction with a malefic planet' as a condition for a specific yoga, implying that checking for conjunctions with malefics is a fundamental validation rule for assessing planetary states and influences.

rules.rule_name: Bhava devoid of benefic conjunction or aspect

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the strength or outcome of a specific Bhava, particularly in matters of health related to pregnancy.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets conjuncting or aspecting the Bhava. Determine if any are natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury associated with benefics). The condition is met if the Bhava is neither conjunct nor aspected by benefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava's indications may suffer or manifest negatively, especially if malefic influences are present, potentially leading to severe outcomes like death during an operation for a pregnant woman.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the first sentence of Verse 13, referring to 'मात्र (Bhava) being neither in conjunction with benefic planets, nor aspected by them'.

rules.rule_name: Mars and Sun in Udaya (rising) and Asta (setting)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing health, particularly regarding potential for injury, surgery, or violent death.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if Mars and Sun are simultaneously in Udaya (rising) and Asta (setting) positions. This typically refers to their placement near the Ascendant and Descendant respectively, or specific angular relationships indicating their visibility and influence at the horizon.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates potential for death by weapon or surgical complications, especially for a pregnant woman.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the Sanskrit text 'उदयास्तगयोः कुजार्कयोर्निधनं शस्त्रकृतं वदेसथा' (If Mars and Sun are in Udaya and Asta, death by weapon should be declared), quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita.

rules.rule_name: Affliction of the Lord of the Month (Pregnancy)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing pregnancy outcomes, especially regarding potential for abortion or complications before 7 months.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet ruling the specific month of pregnancy (as per classical texts). Assess its affliction, which can include conjunction with malefics, aspect from malefics, debilitation, combustion, placement in malefic houses, or being in planetary war.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates potential for abortion or severe complications during that specific month of pregnancy.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the Sanskrit text 'मासाधिपतौ निपीडिते तत्काल स्रवणं समादिशेत्' (If the lord of the month is afflicted, immediate abortion should be declared), quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita.

rules.rule_name: Weakest Planet Determines Timing of Death (Pregnancy)

check_logic.condition: When predicting the specific month of death for a pregnant woman.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planets ruling the ten months of embryo development (as specified in Sloka 17 of the referenced text). Assess the strength of each of these planets using standard strength assessment methods (e.g., Shadbala, dignity, avastha). The planet with the least strength among these rulers will indicate the month of death.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to correctly identify the weakest planet will lead to an incorrect timing prediction for a critical event.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a method for timing based on planetary weakness, requiring an external reference to Sloka 17 for the list of month-ruling planets.

rules.rule_name: Malefics in 4th from Moon without benefic aspect/conjunction

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the mother's longevity, especially immediately after childbirth.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets occupying the 4th house from the natal Moon. Determine if these planets are natural malefics (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury associated with malefics). Simultaneously, check if any natural benefic planets are aspecting or conjuncting the 4th house from the Moon or the malefics within it. The condition is met if malefics are present and no benefics provide mitigating influence.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates potential death of the mother of the newborn child.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from 'When evil planets occupy the 4th place from the Moon and are not aspected by or in conjunction with benefic planets, the mother of the new born child will die.'

rules.rule_name: Malefics in 10th from Moon including Sun

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the mother's longevity, especially immediately after childbirth.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets occupying the 10th house from the natal Moon. Determine if these planets are natural malefics and if the Sun is specifically among them.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates potential death of the mother of the newborn child.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from 'The same event happens when evil planets occupy the 10th place from the Moon and the Sun is among them'.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing matters related to conception, pregnancy, or fetal development.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the sequential planetary lords for the months/stages of fetal development as per the classical text. The text specifies Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Sun, Moon, Mercury, and implies others ('किंबुधाः परतः'). The auspicious or inauspicious nature of the outcome for each month/stage is determined by the nature of its ruling planet ('शुभाशुभं च मासाधिपतेः सदृशम्').

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the nature and outcomes of fetal development stages.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 17
rules.extraction_notes: The text lists a sequence of planets (Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Sun, Moon, Mercury) as lords ('नाथाः क्रमशो गदिताः') for stages of fetal development ('कललधनाङ्करास्थिचर्माङ्गजचेतनताः'). The nature of the outcome ('शुभाशुभं') for each month ('मासाधिपतेः सदृशम्') is determined by its lord. This fixed sequence of rulerships constitutes a table-based rule for a specific analytical context.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and beneficence of any planet, especially in the context of fetal development or general planetary influence.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is in its sign of debilitation (depression sign).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give good results is severely hampered; indicates weakness and suffering related to its significations. For fetal development, this contributes to suffering or miscarriage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'in its depression sign' as a condition for suffering. This is an implicitly table-based rule as debilitation signs are fixed.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and beneficence of any planet, especially Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, and in the context of fetal development or general planetary influence.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is 'conquered in planetary fights' (Graha Yuddha). This typically involves planets being within a specific degree of each other, with one planet being deemed 'conquered' based on specific rules (e.g., lower declination, higher longitude, etc.). The text states 'conquered in planetary fights'.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give good results is severely hampered; indicates weakness and suffering related to its significations. For fetal development, this contributes to suffering or miscarriage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'conquered in planetary fights' as a condition for suffering.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength and Dignity for Fetal Development

check_logic.condition: When assessing the well-being or development of a child during gestation, specifically for each month of pregnancy.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength, dignity (e.g., exaltation, debilitation), and planetary war status of the planetary lord of the specific month of gestation. A strong lord in benefic positions indicates happiness; a weak lord (e.g., in debilitation, conquered in planetary war, or otherwise troubled) indicates suffering.

rules.impact_if_violated: Negative impact on the child's development or well-being during that specific month, potentially leading to suffering or miscarriage.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule synthesizes the conditions for good/evil happening to the child based on the lord of the month's strength, dignity, and planetary war status.

rules.rule_name: Sun's Strength for Resemblance to Father

check_logic.condition: When predicting the physical resemblance of a child to its father.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Sun at the time of conception. 'Strong' implies good dignity, house placement, aspects, etc.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Sun is not strong, the child may not resemble the father, or the resemblance may be weak.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'If, at the time of conception, the Sun is strong, the issue will resemble the father.'

rules.rule_name: Moon's Strength for Resemblance to Mother

check_logic.condition: When predicting the physical resemblance of a child to its mother.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Moon at the time of conception. 'Strong' implies good dignity, house placement, aspects, etc.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Moon is not strong, the child may not resemble the mother, or the resemblance may be weak.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'If the Moon is strong, the issue will resemble the mother.'

rules.rule_name: Infant Mortality Yoga (Saturn, Mars, Sun in 5th)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the natal chart of a new-born infant for longevity.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn, Mars, and Sun are all simultaneously placed in the 5th house from Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify a critical yoga indicating infant mortality.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 24
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific planetary combination (yoga) indicating infant mortality.

check_logic.condition: If the 'Infant Mortality Yoga (Saturn, Mars, Sun in 5th)' rule is confirmed.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 77th Nakshatra reckoned from the birth Nakshatra.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify the specific timing of infant mortality.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 24
rules.extraction_notes: This rule provides the specific timing for the infant mortality indicated by the associated yoga.

rules.rule_name: Fetal Health Impact by Weak Conception Planet

check_logic.condition: A planet is weak at the time of conception.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of all planets at the time of conception using classical strength assessment methods (e.g., Shadbala). Identify planets falling below a minimum strength threshold.

rules.impact_if_violated: The fetus will suffer in health during the month governed by the weak planet.
rules.extraction_notes: General statement at the beginning of the text. Requires definition of 'weak' planet based on other classical texts (e.g., Shadbala). The specific month is determined by the month lordship rules.

check_logic.condition: Determining the planetary lord for the first month of gestation.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet assigned as the lord for the first month according to Garg's view, which is considered authoritative.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planetary influence on the fetus during the first month of development.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Garg's sequence for month 1. Balabhadra states Vasishta's (and thus Garg's) view is authoritative.

check_logic.condition: Determining the planetary lord for the second month of gestation.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet assigned as the lord for the second month according to Garg's view, which is considered authoritative.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planetary influence on the fetus during the second month of development.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Garg's sequence for month 2. Balabhadra states Vasishta's (and thus Garg's) view is authoritative.

check_logic.condition: Determining the planetary lord for the third month of gestation.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet assigned as the lord for the third month according to Garg's view, which is considered authoritative.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planetary influence on the fetus during the third month of development.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Garg's sequence for month 3. Balabhadra states Vasishta's (and thus Garg's) view is authoritative.

check_logic.condition: Determining the planetary lord for the fourth month of gestation.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet assigned as the lord for the fourth month according to Garg's view, which is considered authoritative.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planetary influence on the fetus during the fourth month of development.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Garg's sequence for month 4. Balabhadra states Vasishta's (and thus Garg's) view is authoritative.

check_logic.condition: Determining the planetary lord for the fifth month of gestation.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet assigned as the lord for the fifth month according to Garg's view, which is considered authoritative.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planetary influence on the fetus during the fifth month of development.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Garg's sequence for month 5. Balabhadra states Vasishta's (and thus Garg's) view is authoritative.

check_logic.condition: Determining the planetary lord for the sixth month of gestation.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet assigned as the lord for the sixth month according to Garg's view, which is considered authoritative.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planetary influence on the fetus during the sixth month of development.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Garg's sequence for month 6. Balabhadra states Vasishta's (and thus Garg's) view is authoritative.

rules.rule_name: Body Parts Ruled by First Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When analyzing physical marks or wounds on the body using Drekkana (D3).
check_logic.calculation: The first Drekkana (0-10 degrees) of any sign rules the head, eyes, ears, nose, cheeks, chin, and mouth.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body parts for physical marks/wounds, leading to erroneous predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the first part of the verse describing body parts: 'शिरो दशौ कर्णनसात्र पोलौ हनू तथास्य प्रथमे गाणे'.

rules.rule_name: Body Parts Ruled by Second Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When analyzing physical marks or wounds on the body using Drekkana (D3).
check_logic.calculation: The second Drekkana (10-20 degrees) of any sign rules the throat, shoulders, arms, sides, heart, and navel.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body parts for physical marks/wounds, leading to erroneous predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the second part of the verse describing body parts: 'कण्ठांसकौ बाहुयुगं च पार्श्वे इस्कोडमाभ्यः कथिता द्वितीये'.

rules.rule_name: Body Parts Ruled by Third Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When analyzing physical marks or wounds on the body using Drekkana (D3).
check_logic.calculation: The third Drekkana (20-30 degrees) of any sign rules the bladder, genitals, anus, testicles, thighs, knees, shanks, and feet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of body parts for physical marks/wounds, leading to erroneous predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the third part of the verse describing body parts: 'बस्तिस्तथा शिश्मगुदे थ मुष्काचूरुवयं जानुयुगं च जंघे । पादौ तृतीयेभ्युदितं'.

rules.rule_name: Malefic Planet in Drekkana Indicates Wound/Ulcer

check_logic.condition: When a Drekkana in D3 is occupied by a malefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: Identify malefic planets (e.g., Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, afflicted Mercury). Check if any malefic occupies a Drekkana in D3. If so, a wound or ulcer is indicated in the corresponding body part.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify potential wounds or ulcers on the body, leading to incomplete health analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'तस्मिन् पापयुते व्रणं'.

rules.rule_name: Malefic with Benefic Influence in Drekkana Indicates Spot

check_logic.condition: When a Drekkana occupied by a malefic planet is also occupied or aspected by a benefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: Identify benefic planets (e.g., Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, unafflicted Mercury). Check if a Drekkana with a malefic also has a benefic or is aspected by one. If so, a spot (not a wound/ulcer) is indicated in the corresponding body part.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrectly predicting a wound/ulcer instead of a spot, or missing the indication of a spot, leading to inaccurate physical mark analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'शुभयुते दृष्टे च लक्ष्मादिशेत्'.

check_logic.condition: When determining if a physical mark (wound/spot) is congenital or acquired.
check_logic.calculation: If the planet causing the mark (occupying the Drekkana) is in its own sign (svārthāṃśe) AND the sign it occupies is a fixed sign (sthirasaṃyute), the mark is congenital. Otherwise, it is acquired.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect determination of the origin (congenital/acquired) of a physical mark, affecting the depth of analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'स्वर्थांशे स्थिरसंयुते च सहजः स्यादन्यथागन्तुकः'.

rules.rule_name: Cause of Mark by Saturn in Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When Saturn is the malefic planet indicating a wound/mark in a Drekkana.
check_logic.calculation: If Saturn is the planet causing the mark, its cause is related to stone or wind.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the cause of a physical mark, leading to misinterpretation of the karmic or physical origin.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'मन्देश्मानिलजो'.

rules.rule_name: Cause of Mark by Mars in Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When Mars is the malefic planet indicating a wound/mark in a Drekkana.
check_logic.calculation: If Mars is the planet causing the mark, its cause is related to fire, weapons, or poison.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the cause of a physical mark, leading to misinterpretation of the karmic or physical origin.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'अग्निशस्त्रविषजो भीमे'.

rules.rule_name: Cause of Mark by Mercury in Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When Mercury is the malefic planet indicating a wound/mark in a Drekkana.
check_logic.calculation: If Mercury is the planet causing the mark, its cause is related to earth.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the cause of a physical mark, leading to misinterpretation of the karmic or physical origin.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'बुधे भूभवः'.

rules.rule_name: Cause of Mark by Sun in Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When Sun is the malefic planet indicating a wound/mark in a Drekkana.
check_logic.calculation: If Sun is the planet causing the mark, its cause is related to wood or quadrupeds.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the cause of a physical mark, leading to misinterpretation of the karmic or physical origin.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'सूर्ये काष्ठचतुष्पदेन'.

rules.rule_name: Cause of Mark by Moon in Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When Moon is the malefic planet indicating a wound/mark in a Drekkana.
check_logic.calculation: If Moon is the planet causing the mark (typically when waning or afflicted), its cause is related to horned animals or water-born creatures.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the cause of a physical mark, leading to misinterpretation of the karmic or physical origin.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'हिमगौ श्रृंग्यब्जजोन्यैः'.

rules.rule_name: Left Feet Indicated by Third Drekkana

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the feet for marks or wounds using Drekkana (D3).
check_logic.calculation: The third Drekkana of a sign specifically indicates the left foot.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the side of the body for feet, leading to inaccurate physical mark analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'पादौ तृतीयेभ्युदितं हि वामं'.

rules.rule_name: General Body Side Division by Lagna and 7th House

check_logic.condition: When determining the general side of the body (right/left) for astrological indications.
check_logic.calculation: The Lagna (Ascendant) generally indicates the right side of the body, and the 7th house (the 'other half' opposite Lagna) generally indicates the left side of the body.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect general assignment of body sides for astrological analysis, leading to potential misinterpretations of physical indications.
rules.extraction_notes: Inferred from 'बिलग्नतो दक्षिणमन्यदर्द्धम्' as a general principle of body symmetry in relation to Lagna and its opposite.

rules.rule_name: Infant Mortality: Mars in 7th (Rasi) with Moon's Navamsa, unapected by benefics

check_logic.condition: When assessing the longevity or health of a newborn child.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Check if Mars is placed in the 7th house from Lagna in the Rasi chart. 2. Check if Mars's Navamsa sign (D9 placement) is Cancer (owned by Moon). 3. Check if Mars in the 7th house (Rasi) is NOT aspected by natural benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, or well-placed Mercury).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify this combination may lead to an incorrect assessment of infant longevity, potentially missing a critical indicator of early demise.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Mars in the 7th house (Rasi) IS aspected by natural benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, or well-placed Mercury).
cancellation_conditions.impact: The combination leading to infant death is nullified or significantly mitigated.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific planetary combination involving Rasi and Navamsa charts, indicating infant mortality. The timing aspect (77th Nakshatra) is part of the prediction but the combination itself is the 'promise'.

rules.rule_name: Moon's strength (Akshina Indau) for specific yogas

check_logic.condition: When evaluating yogas related to vitality, conception, or general well-being, especially those mentioned in Brihat Prajapatya.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Moon is waxing (Shukla Paksha) and strong (not in debilitation, not combust, not waning significantly). The term 'अक्षीण इन्दौ' specifically means 'unwaning Moon'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Yogas requiring a strong Moon may not fully fructify or may give adverse results if the Moon is weak/waning.
rules.extraction_notes: Implied rule from the context of a yoga requiring a strong Moon. This is a condition for the yoga's validity.

check_logic.condition: When a yoga uses the term 'अशोभनैः' (asobhanaihi) or 'प/पकैः' (Papakaihi) to refer to malefics.
check_logic.calculation: Interpret 'अशोभनैः' or 'प/पकैः' as meaning 'more than two malefics', i.e., 'three or more malefics'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the number of malefics can lead to incorrect yoga analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the quantity of malefics implied by specific terms in certain yogas, rather than identifying which planets are malefics generally. This definition is then combined with the Rahu/Ketu exclusion for Nishekadhyaya yogas.

rules.rule_name: Rahu/Ketu exclusion from malefics in Nishekadhyaya yogas

check_logic.condition: When identifying 'malefics' for yogas mentioned in the Nishekadhyaya (chapter on conception/birth) as per Skanda Hora.
check_logic.calculation: Do not include Rahu and Ketu in the count of malefics for these specific yogas. In this context, the malefics are Sun, Mars, and Saturn.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrectly including Rahu/Ketu can lead to misinterpretation or misapplication of these specific yogas.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a very specific exclusion rule for a particular set of yogas, defining how to count malefics in that context. It directly states 'Rahu and Ketu are not reckoned as forming part of the malefics in this yoga'.

rules.rule_name: Planets in Apoklima houses are strength-less

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and capacity of a planet to deliver results.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets situated in the 3rd, 6th, 9th, or 12th houses from the Lagna (Apoklima houses).

rules.impact_if_violated: Planets in Apoklima houses are considered to be devoid of great strength, reducing their ability to produce significant or positive results.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from the Sanskrit 'आपोक्किमे स्थिताः सर्वे महा बलविवर्जिताः' (All planets situated in Apoklima are devoid of great strength).

rules.rule_name: Infant mortality due to Moon's affliction and weak Kendras

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a natal chart, particularly for a newborn's longevity.
check_logic.calculation:
Check if ALL the following conditions are met:
1. The Moon occupies the Lagna (rising sign), 6th, 8th, or 12th house.
2. The Moon is aspected by a malefic planet.
3. The Moon is NOT aspected by any benefic planet.
4. The Kendra houses (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) are unoccupied by benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: If all conditions are met, the new-born infant will die, either within a couple of months or six months.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a specific yoga indicating infant mortality. All conditions must be present simultaneously for the rule to apply.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the fundamental strength, health, and overall well-being indicated by the Lagna and its lord.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the sign of the Lagna Lord and check if it is its sign of debilitation (Neecha Rashi).

rules.impact_if_violated: Significantly weakens the significations of the Lagna, impacting the native's overall health, vitality, personality, and general fortune. Can indicate struggles or inherent weaknesses related to the self.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 26
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is extracted as a fundamental component of a critical yoga described in the verse, highlighting its importance as a validation check.

rules.rule_name: Saturn in 7th House from Lagna Check

check_logic.condition: When analyzing relationships, partnerships, longevity, and general well-being, especially concerning the native's interactions with others and life span.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if Saturn is placed in the 7th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Can indicate challenges, delays, or difficulties in marriage and partnerships. May also impact health and longevity, especially in conjunction with other malefic influences.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 26
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is extracted as a fundamental component of a critical yoga described in the verse, highlighting its importance as a validation check for Saturn's placement.

rules.rule_name: Saturn in 8th House from Lagna Check

check_logic.condition: When analyzing longevity, chronic health issues, sudden events, obstacles, and hidden aspects of life.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if Saturn is placed in the 8th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Can indicate challenges with longevity, chronic health problems, sudden misfortunes, or obstacles in life. May also relate to difficulties with inheritance or hidden resources.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 26
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is extracted as a fundamental component of a critical yoga described in the verse, highlighting its importance as a validation check for Saturn's placement.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the Moon's inherent nature, strength, or its role in specific yogas and general chart analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Moon is waxing (Shukla Paksha) or waning (Krishna Paksha) based on its angular distance from the Sun. Waxing Moon is generally considered benefic, while a waning Moon (especially from Krishna Paksha Ashtami to Amavasya) is considered malefic or weak.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Moon's inherent strength and its role as a benefic/malefic, leading to erroneous yoga interpretations and general chart analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 33
rules.extraction_notes: The yoga described in Verse 33 explicitly depends on the Moon being 'waning', which necessitates the determination of the Moon's paksha as a fundamental step in astrological analysis.

rules.rule_name: Benefics in 6th/8th aspected by Malefics for early death

check_logic.condition: Benefic planets occupy the 8th or the 6th house from the Lagna AND are aspected by malefic planets.
check_logic.calculation: Identify benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, strong Moon, unafflicted Mercury). Check their placement in the 6th or 8th house from Lagna. Check if they are aspected by malefic planets (Saturn, Mars, Sun, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, afflicted Mercury).

rules.impact_if_violated: The child dies in a month.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the general introductory text of Verse 22.

rules.rule_name: Waning Moon in Lagna with Malefics in Kendras/8th for early death

check_logic.condition: Waning Moon (Ksheena Shashi) is in the Lagna AND malefic planets are in Kendras (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th houses) or the 8th house.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Moon is waning. Check Moon's placement in the Lagna. Identify malefic planets. Check their placement in Kendras or the 8th house from Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Certain calamity or death occurs.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the first Saravali quote within Verse 22.

rules.rule_name: Waning Moon in Lagna with Malefics in Kendras/8th, un-aspected by Benefics for immediate death

check_logic.condition: Waning Moon (Ksheena Shashi) is in the Lagna AND malefic planets are in Kendras (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th houses) or the 8th house AND these malefics are NOT aspected by benefic planets.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Moon is waning. Check Moon's placement in the Lagna. Identify malefic planets. Check their placement in Kendras or the 8th house from Lagna. Check if any benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, strong Moon, unafflicted Mercury) aspect these malefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: Immediate death occurs.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the second Saravali quote within Verse 22. This refines the previous Saravali rule.

rules.rule_name: Benefics in 6th/8th, aspected by Retrograde Malefics, un-aspected by Benefics for death in a month

check_logic.condition: Benefic planets are in the 6th or 8th house AND are aspected by retrograde malefic planets AND these benefics are NOT aspected by other benefic planets.
check_logic.calculation: Identify benefic planets. Check their placement in the 6th or 8th house from Lagna. Identify malefic planets and check if they are retrograde. Check if these retrograde malefics aspect the benefics in the 6th/8th. Check if any benefic planets aspect the benefics in the 6th/8th.

rules.impact_if_violated: Death occurs within a month.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the third Saravali quote within Verse 22.

rules.rule_name: Mars in 7th and Moon in Lagna for self/parental destruction

check_logic.condition: Mars (Bhūmiputra) is in the 7th house AND the Moon (Chandramāḥ) is in the Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Check Mars' placement in the 7th house from Lagna. Check Moon's placement in the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native kills self, father, and mother.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the first Yavanajataka quote within Verse 22.

rules.rule_name: Rahu in 7th and Moon in Lagna for death in 4th year

check_logic.condition: Rahu is in the 7th house AND the Moon (Chandramāḥ) is in the Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Check Rahu's placement in the 7th house from Lagna. Check Moon's placement in the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Death occurs in the 4th year of life.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the second Yavanajataka quote within Verse 22.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter in 8th/12th, Moon in Lagna, Mars in 8th for early death

check_logic.condition: Jupiter (Jīva) is in the 8th or 12th house AND the Moon (Chandramāḥ) is in the Lagna AND Mars (Maṅgala) is in the 8th house.
check_logic.calculation: Check Jupiter's placement in the 8th or 12th house from Lagna. Check Moon's placement in the Lagna. Check Mars' placement in the 8th house from Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native goes to Yama's abode (death).
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the third Yavanajataka quote within Verse 22.

rules.rule_name: Saturn, Mars, Sun together in 6th/8th, un-conjoined/un-aspected by Benefics for immediate death

check_logic.condition: Saturn, Mars, and the Sun are together (in the same house) AND this conjunction is in the 8th or 6th house from the Lagna AND they are neither in conjunction with benefic planets, nor aspected by them.
check_logic.calculation: Check for conjunction of Saturn, Mars, and Sun. Check if this conjunction is in the 6th or 8th house from Lagna. Check if any benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, strong Moon, unafflicted Mercury) are in conjunction with this trio. Check if any benefic planets aspect this trio.

rules.impact_if_violated: Immediate death of the new-born child.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1, Verse 22 (Sloka 22 summary and Yavanajataka)
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is explicitly stated in the Sloka 22 summary and also in the last Yavanajataka quote, describing the same condition.

rules.rule_name: Infant Mortality Yoga: Moon with Malefics and Specific Placements

check_logic.condition: When assessing the longevity of a new-born infant.
check_logic.calculation: Verify if the Moon is in conjunction with a malefic planet. Then, check if this Moon occupies the Lagna (1st house), 7th, 8th, or 12th house. Additionally, confirm that this Moon is not aspected by any benefic planets. Finally, ensure that benefic planets are not positioned in any of the Kendras (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th houses).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction regarding infant mortality. This combination indicates a high probability of early death for the new-born.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 34
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific yoga for infant mortality. While it's a predictive yoga, it represents a critical, non-negotiable check when analyzing the longevity of a new-born, thus qualifying as a validation rule for that specific context.

check_logic.condition: When assessing Mars's dignity and strength in a birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Mars is placed in the Cancer sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Mars's natural significations (e.g., courage, siblings, property) and house lordships are significantly weakened, corrupted, or cause adverse effects related to its karakatwas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Implied from 'is in his depression' within a predictive context. Mars is debilitated in Cancer.

check_logic.condition: When assessing Mars's dignity and strength in a birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Mars is placed in its own signs, Aries or Scorpio.

rules.impact_if_violated: If Mars is not in its own sign, it lacks the inherent strength and positive influence associated with its own sign placement, potentially weakening its significations.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Implied from 'Rasi owned by Mars' within a predictive context. Mars owns Aries and Scorpio.

check_logic.condition: When assessing Jupiter's dignity and strength in a birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Jupiter is placed in the Capricorn sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Jupiter's natural significations (e.g., wisdom, wealth, children, dharma) and house lordships are significantly weakened, corrupted, or cause adverse effects related to its karakatwas.
rules.extraction_notes: Implied from 'नीचस्थे देवपूज्ये तु' (Jupiter in debilitation) within a predictive context. Jupiter is debilitated in Capricorn.

rules.rule_name: Mercury's Functional Malefic Nature for Specific Yogas

check_logic.condition: When evaluating specific yogas where Mercury's malefic nature is explicitly stated as a prerequisite for the yoga's completion.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the specific yoga being analyzed. Determine if the classical text explicitly states that Mercury must be considered a malefic for that yoga to form or be complete.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific yoga will not form or its effects will be incorrectly assessed, leading to an erroneous prediction.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is stated as a 'N.B.' for specific yogas not detailed in the provided text, but the principle of Mercury's nature being contextually fixed for yoga formation is a validation rule.

rules.rule_name: Arishta Yoga Cancellation - Unaspected Moon in 6th or 8th House

check_logic.condition: An Arishta Yoga (death-causing yoga) is indicated due to the Moon's placement in the 6th or 8th house from Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Moon, when placed in the 6th or 8th house, is not aspected by any other planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Arishta Yoga, potentially predicting death when the yoga is cancelled.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 (commentator Bhattatpala quoting Yavaneswara)
rules.extraction_notes: This is a cancellation condition for Arishta Yogas involving the Moon in the 6th or 8th house.

rules.rule_name: Arishta Yoga Cancellation - Moon in 6th or 8th in Benefic-Owned Rasi

check_logic.condition: An Arishta Yoga (death-causing yoga) is indicated due to the Moon's placement in the 6th or 8th house from Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Moon, when placed in the 6th or 8th house, is situated in a Rasi (sign) owned by a benefic planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Arishta Yoga, potentially predicting death when the yoga is cancelled.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a cancellation condition for Arishta Yogas involving the Moon in the 6th or 8th house.

rules.rule_name: Arishta Yoga Cancellation - Moon in 6th or 8th in Malefic-Owned Rasi with Benefic Conjunction

check_logic.condition: An Arishta Yoga (death-causing yoga) is indicated due to the Moon's placement in the 6th or 8th house from Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Moon, when placed in the 6th or 8th house, is situated in a Rasi (sign) owned by a malefic planet, AND is simultaneously in conjunction with benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Arishta Yoga, potentially predicting death when the yoga is cancelled.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a cancellation condition for Arishta Yogas involving the Moon in the 6th or 8th house.

rules.rule_name: Arishta Yoga Cancellation - Moon in 6th or 8th with Day/Night and Paksha Condition

check_logic.condition: An Arishta Yoga (death-causing yoga) is indicated due to the Moon's placement in the 6th or 8th house from Lagna, and the Moon is aspected by any planet (benefic or malefic).
check_logic.calculation: Check if the birth occurred during the day time in the dark half of the month, OR during the night time in the bright half of the month.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Arishta Yoga, potentially predicting death when the yoga is cancelled.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 (Mandavya's work, referencing Brihat Jataka Chapter XIII. Sloka 8)
rules.extraction_notes: This is a cancellation condition for Arishta Yogas involving the Moon in the 6th or 8th house, incorporating diurnal/nocturnal strength and Paksha Bala.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord Defeated in Planetary War by Malefic

check_logic.condition: Lagna Lord is involved in a planetary war (Graha Yuddha) with a malefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if Lagna Lord is defeated by a malefic planet in Graha Yuddha based on classical rules (e.g., brightness, north declination, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Significantly reduces the native's longevity and overall well-being. Can indicate severe challenges or early demise.
rules.classical_reference: Vasistha (quoted in Jataka Parijata Verse 36 commentary)
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a specific yoga for early death, but the underlying condition (Lagna Lord defeated in planetary war by malefic) is a general planetary state check.

check_logic.condition: Evaluating the nature of the 6th and 8th houses.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 6th and 8th houses from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of house significations, leading to incorrect predictions regarding health, debts, enemies (6th) and longevity, sudden events, occult (8th).
rules.classical_reference: Brihat Jataka XX-10 (quoted in Jataka Parijata Verse 36 commentary)
rules.extraction_notes: Stated as a fundamental principle: 'The 6th and the 8th bhavas are considered bad'.

rules.rule_name: Beneficial Effect of Malefics in 6th/8th Houses

check_logic.condition: Evaluating planetary placements in the 6th and 8th houses.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if malefic planets are placed in the 6th or 8th houses, and if benefic planets are placed there.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Vipareeta Raja Yoga potential and general planetary effects in these challenging houses.
rules.classical_reference: Brihat Jataka XX-10 (quoted in Jataka Parijata Verse 36 commentary)
rules.extraction_notes: Stated as a principle: 'Bad planets in those bhavas are better there than good ones'.

check_logic.condition: Assessing the Moon's overall condition in the chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the Moon's dignity (exaltation, debilitation, own sign, friendly sign, enemy sign) and strength (e.g., Paksha Bala, Shadbala components).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of mental state, emotional well-being, mother's health, general fortune, and the outcome of many yogas.
rules.classical_reference: Shaunaka Hora (quoted in Jataka Parijata Verse 36 commentary)
