rules.impact_if_violated: Missing a significant secondary death-inflicting planet, leading to incomplete longevity analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Stated as 'निधनेश्वरो वा' (or the lord of the 8th) in Phaladeepika.

check_logic.condition: When assessing potential causes of death or critical health periods.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of all planets. A planet that is 'अतिदुर्बलश्चेत्' (very weak) can cause death during its Dasha/Antardasha (स्वपाके). The definition of 'very weak' requires further classical context (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, specific debilitation/combustion rules).

rules.impact_if_violated: Overlooking a weak planet's potential to cause harm, leading to an incomplete assessment of life threats.
rules.extraction_notes: The term 'अतिदुर्बलश्चेत्' (if very weak) implies a critical strength threshold, but no numerical value is provided in this text snippet.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Transit for Death Timing (Trimsamsha of 8th Lord)

check_logic.condition: When attempting to time the period of death or a severe life crisis.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the lord of the 8th house in the Rashi chart. 2. Determine the Trimsamsha sign of this 8th house lord. 3. Death can occur when Saturn transits the Rashi house corresponding to the sign occupied by the lord of the 8th house's Trimsamsha.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify a specific and potent timing indicator for death, leading to inaccurate event timing.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule involves the Trimsamsha divisional chart and Saturn's transit. 'अष्टमस्थत्रिभागांशपतिस्थितगृहं शनौ' (When Saturn goes to the house occupied by the lord of the 8th house's Trimsamsha).

rules.rule_name: Saturn Transit for Death Timing (Navamsha of 8th Lord)

check_logic.condition: When attempting to time the period of death or a severe life crisis.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the lord of the 8th house in the Rashi chart. 2. Determine the Navamsha sign of this 8th house lord. 3. Death can occur when Saturn transits the Rashi sign corresponding to the Navamsha sign of the 8th house lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify a specific and potent timing indicator for death, leading to inaccurate event timing.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule involves the Navamsha divisional chart and Saturn's transit. 'तदीशनवभागक्षं गते वा मरणं भवेत्' (Or when Saturn goes to the Navamsha sign of that lord, death occurs).

rules.rule_name: Mandatory Planetary Strength Comparison for Timing Relative's Death

check_logic.condition: When predicting the timing of death for a relative signified by a specific bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the Veerya (strength) of the planet owning the bhava (Bhava Lord) and the planet occupying the 12th house from that bhava. Identify which of these two planets is stronger.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to correctly identify the stronger planet will lead to an incorrect selection of the operative Dasa, rendering the timing prediction for the relative's death erroneous.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 54
rules.extraction_notes: This rule mandates a strength comparison between the Bhava Lord and the planet in the 12th from the Bhava to identify the stronger planet, which is a prerequisite for timing the death of the relative signified by that Bhava.

rules.rule_name: Timing Relative's Death in Dasa of Stronger Bhava Lord or 12th Occupant

check_logic.condition: After performing the mandatory planetary strength comparison between the Bhava Lord and the planet in the 12th from the bhava (as per Jataka Parijata 54) and identifying the stronger planet.
check_logic.calculation: The death of the relative signified by the particular bhava will occur without doubt in the main Dasa of the planet previously identified as stronger (either the Bhava Lord or the planet occupying the 12th from the Bhava).

rules.impact_if_violated: Ignoring the Dasa of the stronger planet will result in an incorrect timing prediction for the relative's death.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 54
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies that the Dasa of the stronger planet (determined by a prior strength assessment) is the operative period for the death of the relative signified by the bhava.

check_logic.condition: When identifying sensitive points or assessing potential challenges related to the 8th house from Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: The 22nd Drekkana counted from the Lagna Drekkana. This corresponds to the first Drekkana of the 8th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misidentification of a sensitive divisional point, potentially leading to incorrect assessment of challenges or longevity.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Khara Drekkana as the 22nd from Lagna Drekkana, which is the 1st Drekkana of the 8th house from Lagna.

check_logic.condition: When identifying sensitive points or assessing potential challenges related to the 8th house from Moon.
check_logic.calculation: The 64th Navamsa counted from the Navamsa occupied by the Moon. This corresponds to the first Navamsa of the 8th house from the Moon.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misidentification of a sensitive divisional point, potentially leading to incorrect assessment of challenges or longevity.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Defines 64th Navamsa as the 64th from Moon's Navamsa, which is the 1st Navamsa of the 8th house from Moon.

check_logic.condition: When assessing vitality, life force, or longevity.
check_logic.calculation: Prana = (Lagna_longitude_in_degrees * 5) + Mandi_longitude_in_degrees. (All longitudes should be in degrees from Aries 0).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Prana, leading to an inaccurate assessment of life force and vitality, which impacts longevity predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text states 'figures for the rising sign multiplied by 5'. This is interpreted as Lagna longitude in degrees. Mandi's position is required.

check_logic.condition: When assessing physical body strength, vitality, or longevity.
check_logic.calculation: Deha = (Moon_longitude_in_degrees * 8) + Mandi_longitude_in_degrees. (All longitudes should be in degrees from Aries 0).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Deha, leading to an inaccurate assessment of physical body strength and vitality, which impacts longevity predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text states 'figures for the Moon multiplied by 8'. This is interpreted as Moon's longitude in degrees. Mandi's position is required.

check_logic.condition: When assessing factors related to death or challenges to longevity.
check_logic.calculation: Mrityu = (Mandi_longitude_in_degrees * 7) + Sun_longitude_in_degrees. (All longitudes should be in degrees from Aries 0).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Mrityu, leading to an inaccurate assessment of factors related to death and challenges to longevity.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text states 'Seven times the figures for Mandi joined to those of the Sun'. This is interpreted as Mandi's longitude multiplied by 7 and added to Sun's longitude in degrees. Mandi's position is required.

rules.rule_name: Longevity Assessment based on Prana, Deha, Mrityu

check_logic.condition: When making a longevity prediction.
check_logic.calculation: Compare the sum of Prana and Deha with Mrityu. Long life is indicated if (Prana + Deha) > Mrityu.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of longevity, leading to potentially grave mispredictions about life span or significant health challenges.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule states the condition for 'long life'. The opposite condition would imply shorter life or significant challenges to longevity. Requires prior calculation of Prana, Deha, and Mrityu.

rules.rule_name: Identify Chhidra Grahas for Death Prediction (Jataka Parijata)

check_logic.condition: When predicting potential periods of death according to Jataka Parijata.
check_logic.calculation:
Identify the following seven planets/entities as Chhidra Grahas:
1. The lord of the 8th bhava.
2. The planet occupying the 8th bhava.
3. The planet aspecting the 8th bhava.
4. The lord of the 22nd Drekkana from the Lagna Drekkana (Khara Drekkana).
5. The planet in conjunction with the lord of the 8th bhava.
6. The lord of the 64th Navamsa from the Navamsa occupied by the Moon.
7. The Atishatru (greatest enemy) of the lord of the 8th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of potential death-giving planets, leading to flawed analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the specific set of 'Chhidra Grahas' as per Jataka Parijata, which are crucial for subsequent death timing analysis.

rules.rule_name: Predict Death from Dasa of Strongest Chhidra Graha (Jataka Parijata)

check_logic.condition: After identifying all Chhidra Grahas as per Jataka Parijata Verse 53.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the strongest planet among the identified seven Chhidra Grahas. The dasa of this strongest Chhidra Graha should be predicted as the period of death.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of death prediction due to misapplication of strength criteria.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies that the strongest among the Chhidra Grahas determines the timing of death, a key principle in Jataka Parijata.

rules.rule_name: Identify Death-Giving Planets (Mantreswara/Prashna Marga - Weakest Principle)

check_logic.condition: When considering alternative classical views for identifying death-giving planets, particularly those emphasizing weakness and affliction.
check_logic.calculation:
Identify the following planets/entities as potential death-givers:
1. Mandi
2. Gulika
3. Lord of Khara Drekkana (22nd Drekkana from Lagna)
4. Lord of the 8th house (Mruthipathi)
5. Lord of the 64th Navamsa from the Moon's Navamsa
6. Planet occupying the 8th house (Mruthyustha)
7. Planet aspecting the 8th house (Thadveekshakau)
8. Lord of Krura Drekkana (malefic Drekkana)
9. Planets conjoined with any of the above (1-8)
10. Rahu

rules.impact_if_violated: Missing potential death-giving planets or misinterpreting their roles based on this specific school of thought.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule compiles potential death-giving planets from Mantreswara and Prashna Marga, which often lead to the 'weakest' planet for death timing, contrasting with Jataka Parijata's 'strongest'.

rules.rule_name: Predict Death from Dasa/Antardasa of Weakest Afflicted Death-Giving Planet (Mantreswara/Prashna Marga)

check_logic.condition: After identifying the death-giving planets as per Mantreswara/Prashna Marga, and when considering affliction.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the weakest planet among the identified death-giving planets. If this weakest planet is afflicted by malefics (Papa-alekita-samyuto), its dasa or antardasa indicates the period of death.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of death prediction due to misapplication of strength and affliction criteria.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies that the weakest among the identified death-giving planets, especially if afflicted, determines the timing of death, presenting a contrasting view to Jataka Parijata's 'strongest'.

rules.rule_name: Predict Death from Simultaneous Dasa Endings (Prashna Marga)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing potential periods of death, especially considering specific timing techniques.
check_logic.calculation: Identify periods where two or more dasas (e.g., Dasa and Antardasa, or multiple planetary dasas in a specific system) end simultaneously. Such a period is considered a potential time of death (Kala). The ending of all dasas simultaneously is particularly inauspicious and strongly indicates death.

rules.impact_if_violated: Missing a significant indicator for death timing based on simultaneous astrological period terminations.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule highlights a specific timing technique for death prediction based on the simultaneous termination of multiple dasas, as mentioned in Prashna Marga.

check_logic.condition: When Gulika's position is required for analysis during daytime birth.
check_logic.calculation: Divide the day into 8 equal parts. The lords of the first 7 parts are the 7 planets (Sun to Saturn) counted in order from the lord of the day. The 8th part is lordless. Gulika's portion is Saturn's portion.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Gulika's position, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Procedural rule for calculating Gulika during the day.

check_logic.condition: When Gulika's position is required for analysis during nighttime birth.
check_logic.calculation: Divide the night into 8 equal portions (muhurtas). The lords of the first 7 muhurtas are the 7 planets counted from the lord of the fifth weekday from the day chosen. The 8th portion is lordless. Gulika's muhurta is Saturn's muhurta.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Gulika's position, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Procedural rule for calculating Gulika during the night.

check_logic.condition: When Yamakantaka's position is required for analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Yamakantaka's muhurta is Jupiter's muhurta, determined by the same day/night division rules as Gulika.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Yamakantaka's position, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Procedural rule for calculating Yamakantaka.

check_logic.condition: When Mrityu's position is required for analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Mrityu's muhurta is Mars' muhurta, determined by the same day/night division rules as Gulika.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Mrityu's position, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Procedural rule for calculating Mrityu.

check_logic.condition: When Kala's position is required for analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Kala's muhurta is the Sun's muhurta, determined by the same day/night division rules as Gulika.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Kala's position, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Procedural rule for calculating Kala.

check_logic.condition: When Ardhaprahara's position is required for analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Ardhaprahara's muhurta is Mercury's muhurta, determined by the same day/night division rules as Gulika.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Ardhaprahara's position, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Procedural rule for calculating Ardhaprahara.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the precise zodiacal position of Gulika, Yamakantaka, Mrityu, Kala, or Ardhaprahara.
check_logic.calculation: The position of Gulika and other sensitive points is assigned at the end of their respective muhurtas.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect zodiacal position for sensitive points, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assigning the exact zodiacal position of sensitive points based on their muhurta.

check_logic.condition: When Prana is required for analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Prana is calculated as (Lagna longitude * 5) + Mandi longitude.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Prana, leading to erroneous astrological analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Specific formula for Prana calculation given in the example section, where Mandi is synonymous with Gulika.

check_logic.condition: When a planet is 'eclipsed by the Sun'.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is within the orb of combustion with the Sun. (Specific orb not provided in this text, but implied by 'eclipsed').

rules.impact_if_violated: The combust planet loses its ability to give full or auspicious results, becoming weak or malefic in its indications, potentially leading to adverse outcomes related to its significations and lordship.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 82
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the phrase 'eclipsed by the Sun', which is a classical description of planetary combustion. The specific angular threshold for combustion is not provided in this verse.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Transit for Financial Loss (Deha+Jeeva+Mrityu Rasi)

check_logic.condition: Saturn transits the Rasi (sign) derived from the sum of Deha, Jeeva, and Mrityu.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the sum of the longitudes of Deha, Jeeva, and Mrityu. Determine the Rasi corresponding to this total longitude. Check if Saturn's current transit Rasi matches this calculated Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction regarding financial loss.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 58
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for predicting financial loss based on Saturn's transit to a specific calculated sign. The calculation of Deha, Jeeva, and Mrityu is a prerequisite.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Transit for Death (Triangular/Navamsa from Deha+Jeeva+Mrityu Rasi)

check_logic.condition: Saturn transits a Rasi that is a trine (5th or 9th) from the (Deha + Jeeva + Mrityu) Rasi, OR Saturn transits a Navamsa sign whose lord is the lord of the (Deha + Jeeva + Mrityu) Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the (Deha + Jeeva + Mrityu) Rasi. Identify the Rasis that are 5th and 9th from it. Identify the lord of the (Deha + Jeeva + Mrityu) Rasi. Check if Saturn's transit Rasi is one of the trines, or if Saturn's transit Navamsa sign is owned by the lord of the (Deha + Jeeva + Mrityu) Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction regarding death.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 58
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for predicting death based on Saturn's transit to a trine or Navamsa related to a specific calculated sign (Deha + Jeeva + Mrityu Rasi).

check_logic.condition: Saturn transits a Rasi that is a trine (5th or 9th) from the Ayurbhava (8th house) Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Rasi of the 8th house (Ayurbhava). Identify the Rasis that are 5th and 9th from it. Check if Saturn's current transit Rasi matches one of these trine Rasis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction regarding death.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 59
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for predicting death based on Saturn's transit to a trine of the 8th house (Ayurbhava).

rules.rule_name: Jupiter Transit for Death (Ayurbhava Lord's Rasi Trine)

check_logic.condition: Jupiter transits a Rasi that is a trine (5th or 9th) from the Rasi occupied by the lord of the Ayurbhava (8th house).
check_logic.calculation: Identify the lord of the 8th house (Ayurbhava). Determine the Rasi occupied by this lord. Identify the Rasis that are 5th and 9th from this occupied Rasi. Check if Jupiter's current transit Rasi matches one of these trine Rasis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction regarding death.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 59
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for predicting death based on Jupiter's transit to a trine of the Rasi occupied by the 8th house lord.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing potential causes of death or severe life events.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the lord of the 10th house in the Navamsa (D9) chart. Check if this lord is in conjunction with Rahu or Ketu in the Rashi (D1) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of potential cause of death related to hanging.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 87
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from a specific predictive statement. The conjunction location (Rashi vs. Navamsa) is not explicitly stated for the conjunction, but typically divisional lord's placement/conjunctions are seen in Rashi or the divisional chart itself. Assuming Rashi for conjunction based on common practice unless specified.

rules.rule_name: Waning Moon Conjunction with Malefics in 8th Bhava

check_logic.condition: When analyzing potential troubles, dangers, or health issues.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Determine if the Moon is waning (Krishna Paksha). 2. Check if the Moon is in conjunction with Mars, Rahu, or Saturn. 3. Check if this conjunction occurs in the 8th bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify potential troubles from demoniacal possession, fire, or water.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 86
rules.extraction_notes: Combines Moon's state with planetary conjunction and house placement.

rules.rule_name: Weak Sun/Mars in 8th with Malefics in 2nd

check_logic.condition: When analyzing potential causes of death or severe health issues.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Assess the strength of the Sun and Mars (e.g., using Shadbala, Avasthas, etc.). 2. Check if either Sun or Mars, lacking strength, occupies the 8th bhava. 3. Check if malefic planets (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, combust planets) occupy the 2nd bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of potential cause of death related to bilious complaints.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 87
rules.extraction_notes: The term 'without strength' implies a need for a comprehensive strength assessment (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas). The text does not specify the exact numerical threshold for 'without strength'.

rules.rule_name: Sthana Bala from Rising Navamsa Lord Association

check_logic.condition: When assessing the Sthana Bala (positional strength) of a planet.
check_logic.calculation: A planet gains Sthana Bala if it is in conjunction with or aspected by the lord of the rising Navamsa (Navamsa Lagna lord).

rules.impact_if_violated: Inaccurate assessment of a planet's positional strength (Sthana Bala), leading to an incorrect overall Shadbala calculation and potentially flawed predictive outcomes regarding the planet's capacity to deliver its results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific factor contributing to Sthana Bala, related to the rising Navamsa lord's association with other planets. It is mentioned as an introductory statement before the numbered verse 117.

check_logic.condition: Before any astrological analysis involving houses, their significations, or planetary rulerships.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the lord of each bhava based on the sign occupying the cusp of that bhava. This is typically derived from a fixed table of sign lords.

rules.impact_if_violated: Fundamental misinterpretation of planetary roles, house significations, and the relationships between different areas of life.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 85, 84
rules.extraction_notes: The verses implicitly require the identification of house lords (e.g., 'lords of the 8th and 1st bhava', 'lord of the Lagna', 'lord of the 4th bhava', 'lord of the 7th bhava') to form the stated combinations and conditions.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the malefic or challenging influences in a chart, or the placement of planets in adverse houses.
check_logic.calculation: Identify houses 6, 8, and 12 as Dusthanas (malefic houses). Some traditions also include house 3.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of challenging periods, obstacles, diseases, debts, or areas of life associated with loss and transformation.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The un-numbered verse uses 'दुःस्थानगो' (in a Dusthana) as a key condition for a specific outcome, implying the need to identify these houses as a fundamental step.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing planetary interactions and their combined effects on houses or other planets.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets placed in the same sign or within a specified orb of conjunction (orb not specified in this text, but generally accepted in astrology).

rules.impact_if_violated: Missing significant planetary interactions that profoundly influence outcomes and specific events.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 85, 84
rules.extraction_notes: Multiple verses use 'conjunction' as a key condition for specific predictions, establishing it as a fundamental check.

rules.rule_name: Assessment of Lagna Lord and 8th Lord Strength

check_logic.condition: When assessing longevity, health, or critical life events.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the strength (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, placement) of the Lagna Lord and 8th Lord. The text specifically mentions 'weak'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of longevity, vitality, or potential for critical life events.

rules.extraction_notes: The text implies that weakness of these lords is a critical factor for specific outcomes, thus requiring their strength assessment as a fundamental check.

check_logic.condition: To assess the overall vitality and fortune of the native.
check_logic.calculation: Evaluate the strength of the Lagna Lord based on its dignity, Shadbala, Avasthas, and placement.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the native's fundamental vitality, health, and ability to achieve goals.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 110
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is implied from the condition 'lord of the rising sign is deficient in strength' mentioned in the context of a specific death yoga. It highlights the necessity of assessing Lagna Lord's strength as a fundamental check, even if a specific numerical threshold for 'deficient' is not provided here.

check_logic.condition: To determine the Moon's inherent benefic or malefic nature and strength.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Moon is waxing (Shukla Paksha) or waning (Krishna Paksha). A waning Moon is generally considered weaker or more malefic.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the Moon's influence on mind, emotions, and general well-being.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 108
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is implied from the condition 'waning Moon' mentioned in the context of a specific death yoga. It indicates that the Moon's paksha is a necessary factor to consider in astrological analysis.

rules.rule_name: 8th House Unoccupied and Aspected by Strong Planet for Death Analysis

check_logic.condition: To apply the specific interpretations of Sloka 1 regarding the humour, disease, or manner of death.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Verify if the 8th house is unoccupied by any planet. 2. If unoccupied, identify all planets casting an aspect on the 8th house. 3. Assess the strength (e.g., Shadbala, positional strength, temporal strength) of each aspecting planet. The rule specifically requires the aspecting planet(s) to be 'strong'.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific interpretation regarding the humour, disease, or manner of death derived from this sloka (Sloka 1) will not be applicable or will be significantly altered.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 1
rules.extraction_notes: This rule combines two conditions (unoccupied house and strong aspect) as presented in the classical text for a specific interpretive context related to death. The 'strong planet' aspect directly relates to strength assessment.

check_logic.condition: Always applies to a birth chart to determine specific inauspicious time periods at birth.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the Vishaghatika period for the birth moment. The calculation involves the Nakshatra, its elapsed or remaining portion, and specific Dhruva (constant) values. The formula is: (Nakshatra's elapsed/remaining portion * Dhruva) / 60. The Dhruva values are provided in the text as: पञ्चाश १० ज्जिन २४ खाग्नय ३० श्च खकृता ४० आखण्डला १४ मूच्छेना २१ विश ३० द्विश २० रदाः ३२ खराम ३० नख २० भृत्ये १८ काश्विनौ २१ विंशतिः २०॥ शर्के १४ द्रौ १४ दश १० वासवा १४ रक्तशराः १६ सिद्धा २४ नखा २० शा १० दिशो १० धृत्य १८ ष्टी १६ जिन २४ खाग्नयो ३० श्वितइमाभ्योऽयेऽच्चिनाख्यो विषम् ॥

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to calculate Vishaghatika may lead to incomplete or inaccurate predictions regarding the native's longevity and potential cause of death, especially concerning poison or firearms.
rules.extraction_notes: The text provides numerical values (Dhruva) and a formula for calculating Vishaghatika. The specific Dhruva values are listed in Sanskrit numerals and words. The rule is derived from the context of its predictive impact on death.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and nature of planets, especially the 8th lord, or the Lagna, to identify if they fall into specific 'poisonous' divisions within signs.
check_logic.calculation:
Identify if a planet or Lagna falls into a 'poisonous' Navamsha (Vishamsha) based on the sign it occupies and the Navamsha division. The poisonous Navamshas are categorized as Bhujanga, Gridhra, and Varaha Vish, corresponding to specific Navamshas within certain signs:
*   **Bhujanga Vish (Beginning - 1st Navamsha):** Applies if the planet/Lagna is in the 1st Navamsha of Aries (मेष), Taurus (वृषभ), or Virgo (कन्या).
*   **Gridhra Vish (Middle - 5th Navamsha):** Applies if the planet/Lagna is in the 5th Navamsha of Leo (सिंह), Libra (तुला), or Scorpio (वृश्चिक).
*   **Varaha Vish (End - 9th Navamsha):** Applies if the planet/Lagna is in the 9th Navamsha of Pisces (मीन), Aquarius (कुंभ), or Scorpio (वृश्चिक).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify Vishamsha may lead to incomplete or inaccurate predictions regarding the native's health, longevity, and cause of death, especially if the 8th lord is involved or if the Lagna falls in such a division.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1, Chapter 5, Sloka 112, also Kalpakashika 8.30, Horasara
rules.extraction_notes: The text specifies 'आदौ मध्येऽम्तिमे विषम्' (beginning, middle, end poison) and 'राशिष्वेषु नर्वाशकम्' (in these signs, Navamsha). This implies specific Navamsha divisions. The interpretation of 'beginning', 'middle', 'end' as 1st, 5th, and 9th Navamsha respectively is a common understanding in classical texts for such divisions. Scorpio is listed for both middle (Gridhra Vish) and end (Varaha Vish) Navamshas.

rules.rule_name: Randhranavamsa Calculation for Place of Death

check_logic.condition: When determining the place of death.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the 61st Navamsa from the Lagna Navamsa. This 61st Navamsa is referred to as the Randhranavamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the Randhranavamsa, leading to an erroneous prediction of the place of death.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1, Adhyaya 5. A. Sloka. 12 (implied by reference)
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies a mandatory calculation step for predicting the place of death.

rules.rule_name: Interpretation of Randhranavamsa Sign for Place of Death

check_logic.condition: After identifying the Randhranavamsa sign for predicting the place of death.
check_logic.calculation: Refer to the characteristics and 'haunts' (natural environments/places) associated with the sign that owns the Randhranavamsa, as described in Adhyaya 1, Sloka 10-12.

rules.impact_if_violated: Inaccurate or incomplete description of the place of death.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1, Adhyaya 1. Sl. 10-12 (implied by reference)
rules.extraction_notes: This rule mandates a cross-reference to another section of the text to interpret the nature of the Randhranavamsa sign for predicting the place of death.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 114
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies a cause of death (fever, respiratory issues, pain, headache) when the Lagna Lord is in Gemini Navamsa. 'Tumbura' and 'Yugma' are identified as Gemini.

rules.rule_name: Mitigation of Malefic Yogas by Benefic Aspects

check_logic.condition: When a malefic planet is involved in a yoga leading to a negative outcome (e.g., imprisonment, death).
check_logic.calculation: Check if the malefic planet receives aspects from any benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury if unafflicted).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the yoga's manifestation; potential overestimation of negative outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is inferred from the condition 'provided the malefic planet is not aspected by benefics' which acts as a mitigating factor for a specific death-in-imprisonment yoga. It represents a general principle of benefic aspects mitigating malefic effects.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the cause or timing of death, or general longevity.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 22nd decanate from the Lagna decanate. Identify the lord of this 22nd decanate.

rules.impact_if_violated: Crucial indicators for the cause and nature of death will be missed, leading to an incomplete or inaccurate analysis of longevity and death.
rules.extraction_notes: The 22nd decanate and its lord are declared as primary indicators for the cause of death. This is a fundamental rule for death analysis, identifying a specific divisional component as a key factor.

check_logic.condition: Always
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the angular distance between the Moon and the Sun. If Moon's longitude is greater than Sun's longitude by 180 degrees up to 360 degrees (or 0 degrees), it is waning (Krishna Paksha). If Moon's longitude is greater than Sun's longitude by 0 degrees up to 180 degrees, it is waxing (Shukla Paksha).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Moon's strength, benefic/malefic nature, and overall impact on the chart, especially for yogas where Moon's paksha is a specific condition.
rules.classical_reference: Implied by the condition 'waning Moon' in predictive yogas across classical texts (e.g., BPHS, Saravali). The specific verse uses 'waning Moon' as a critical condition.
rules.extraction_notes: The explicit mention of 'waning Moon' as a critical condition for a specific yoga highlights the fundamental importance of determining the Moon's paksha in any astrological analysis, as it significantly impacts the Moon's strength and results.

rules.rule_name: Nature of Death/Transformation based on 8th House Decanate

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the nature of death, longevity, or significant life transformations.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the decanate (D3) of the 8th bhava. Determine its elemental nature (fiery, watery, earthy, airy). The text specifically mentions 'fiery, watery, or mixed'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the nature of death or significant life transformations.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of the 8th bhava's decanate and its implications for transformation/death. The term 'mixed' in the text likely refers to non-fiery/non-watery elemental decanates (earthy/airy).

check_logic.condition: When assessing the nature of the afterlife based on the 12th bhava.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the lord of the 12th bhava based on the Lagna. 2. Determine the Shashtyamsa (D60) occupied by the 12th lord. 3. Classify the specific Shashtyamsa as malefic or benefic according to classical definitions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the dying person's destination (e.g., hell).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of house lordship rules and Shashtyamsa classifications (malefic/benefic).

rules.rule_name: Malefic Aspect on 12th Lord in Malefic Shashtyamsa

check_logic.condition: When the lord of the 12th bhava occupies a malefic Shashtyamsa.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify malefic planets (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu, functional malefics). 2. Check if the 12th lord (which is in a malefic Shashtyamsa) is aspected by any of these malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the dying person's destination (e.g., hell).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of planetary aspects and classification of planets as malefic.

rules.rule_name: Rahu in 12th with Mandi and 8th Lord Conjunction

check_logic.condition: When assessing the nature of the afterlife.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Check if Rahu is placed in the 12th bhava. 2. Check if Mandi is in conjunction with Rahu in the 12th bhava. 3. Identify the lord of the 8th bhava based on the Lagna and check if it is in conjunction with Rahu and Mandi in the 12th bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the dying person's destination (e.g., hell).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of house lordship rules and Mandi's calculation/placement.

rules.rule_name: Aspect of 6th Lord on Rahu-Mandi-8th Lord Conjunction

check_logic.condition: When Rahu, Mandi, and the 8th lord are conjoined in the 12th bhava.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the lord of the 6th bhava based on the Lagna. 2. Check if the 6th lord aspects the conjunction of Rahu, Mandi, and the 8th lord in the 12th bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the dying person's destination (e.g., hell).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of house lordship rules and planetary aspects.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Planet Exalted in Benefic Varga in 12th

check_logic.condition: When assessing celestial happiness after death.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify benefic planets (e.g., Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, unafflicted Mercury). 2. Check if any benefic planet is in its sign of exaltation. 3. Check if this exalted benefic planet occupies a benefic Varga (e.g., Shashtyamsa, Navamsa, etc., as per classical definitions of 'Varga'). 4. Check if this planet is placed in the 12th bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of celestial happiness.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of planetary exaltation signs, classification of planets as benefic, and classification of Vargas as benefic.

rules.rule_name: Aspect on Exalted Benefic in Benefic Varga in 12th

check_logic.condition: When a benefic planet is exalted in a benefic Varga and placed in the 12th bhava.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify benefic and malefic planets. 2. Check if the benefic planet (which is exalted in a benefic Varga in the 12th bhava) is aspected by both benefic and malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of celestial happiness.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of planetary aspects and classification of planets as benefic/malefic.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the attainment of immortality after death.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify Jupiter. 2. Determine if Jupiter is the lord of the 10th bhava for the given Lagna. 3. Check if Jupiter (as 10th lord) is placed in the 12th bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of attaining immortality.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of house lordship rules based on Lagna.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Aspect on Jupiter (10th Lord in 12th)

check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify benefic planets. 2. Check if Jupiter (as 10th lord in 12th) is aspected by any benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of attaining immortality.

rules.extraction_notes: Requires prior knowledge of planetary aspects and classification of planets as benefic.

rules.rule_name: Precedence of Specific Death Yogas over 22nd Decanate

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the cause of death or severe disease.
check_logic.calculation: First check for specific death-causing yogas mentioned in Slokas 2 to 10 (as referenced in the text). If such yogas are present, their principles take precedence.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect primary cause of death/disease identified, leading to misinterpretation of the chart's promise.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from 'In the absence of the yogas mentioned in Slokas 2 to 10, we have to apply this principle and not otherwise.'

rules.rule_name: Identification of 22nd Decanate as Primary Indicator for Cause of Death/Disease

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the cause of death or severe disease, especially if specific yogas (Slokas 2-10) are absent or inconclusive.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 22nd decanate (drekkana) from the Lagna drekkana. The ruler of this 22nd decanate is a primary determinant for the nature of disease or death.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify this primary indicator will lead to a fundamental misdiagnosis of the cause of death/disease.
rules.extraction_notes: Confirmed by multiple classical references in the text, stating 'The 22nd decanate from the Lagna drekkana causes the disease according to the nature of the ruler of that decanate with certainty.'

rules.rule_name: Modification of 22nd Decanate Influence by 8th House Ruler

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the cause of death/disease using the 22nd decanate.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the ruler of the 8th house. The nature and characteristics of this planet will modify the influence indicated by the ruler of the 22nd decanate.

rules.impact_if_violated: Inaccurate or incomplete assessment of the cause of death/disease, missing crucial modifying factors.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly stated: 'The ruler of the 8th house modifies the influence of the 22nd decanate.'

rules.rule_name: Sun as 22nd Decanate Ruler Indicates Fever

check_logic.condition: If the Sun is identified as the ruler of the 22nd decanate.
check_logic.calculation: The cause of disease or death will be related to fever, as per classical texts.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrectly identifying the specific nature of disease/death based on planetary rulership.
rules.extraction_notes: Specific example given in the text: 'If the ruler of the 22nd decanate be the Sun, he causes fever as stated in Sloka 1.'

rules.rule_name: Moon as 22nd Decanate Ruler Indicates Water Diseases

check_logic.condition: If the Moon is identified as the ruler of the 22nd decanate.
check_logic.calculation: The cause of disease or death will be related to water diseases (e.g., loose bowels, urinal complaints).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrectly identifying the specific nature of disease/death based on planetary rulership.
rules.extraction_notes: Specific example given in the text: 'The Moon gives water diseases...'

rules.rule_name: Mars as 8th House Ruler Modifies Death to Accidental/Short

check_logic.condition: If Mars is identified as the ruler of the 8th house, modifying the influence of the 22nd decanate.
check_logic.calculation: The nature of death will be accidental, unexpected, or short.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrectly assessing the modifying factor for death/disease, leading to an inaccurate prediction of its nature.
rules.extraction_notes: Specific example given in the text: 'The ruler of the 8th house is Mars. Therefore death is accidental, unexpected or short because it is Mars.'

rules.rule_name: Strength assessment for determining post-mortem destination

check_logic.condition: When determining the world to which the departed person goes
check_logic.calculation: Compare the strength of the lord of the Drekkana of the 6th house, the lord of the Drekkana of the 8th house, the planet occupying the 7th bhava, the planet occupying the 6th bhava, and the planet occupying the 8th bhava. The strongest among these determines the outcome.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect determination of the world of the departed due to failure to identify the strongest planet.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 15
rules.extraction_notes: This rule mandates a comparative strength assessment among a specific set of planets to determine the post-mortem state.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the promise of final emancipation (Moksha)
check_logic.calculation: Check if Jupiter is in its sign of exaltation.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify a specific condition for final emancipation.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 15
rules.extraction_notes: This is one of the conditions for final emancipation. It must be combined with a house placement condition for Jupiter.

check_logic.condition: Always applies for any planetary analysis.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is exalted (उच्च) or debilitated (नीच) based on its sign placement according to classical dignity tables.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of a planet's inherent strength and its ability to deliver results, leading to flawed predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Sanskrit verse 'उत्कृष्ठमध्याधमता च तेषामुचच्युते नीचसमाश्रिते च' which explicitly links excellent/medium/inferior states to exaltation and debilitation, and also from the mention of 'Exalted Jupiter' in the English text.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dominance of planets, their ability to influence outcomes, or specific conditions requiring comparative strength.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the strength (e.g., Shadbala, Veerya) of relevant planets and compare their values to determine relative power. The text implies a comparison where one planet's strength should not be surpassed by others for certain outcomes.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misjudgment of which planet's influence will dominate, leading to incorrect predictions about outcomes and timing of events.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1, Text and Sanskrit Verse
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by the condition 'the other planets must not be powerful as Jupiter' for a specific outcome, and the Sanskrit verse mentioning 'वीर्य' (strength) and various states (उत्कृष्ठमध्याधमता).

check_logic.condition: When assessing the deeper potential, marital prospects, spiritual inclinations, or general fortune of the native.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Lagna (ascendant) of the Navamsa (D9) chart. Determine the lord of this Navamsa Lagna. Assess whether this lord is a natural benefic or a natural malefic.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete or inaccurate assessment of the native's deeper potential, marital life, spiritual path, and the quality of results promised by the Rashi chart.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition 'If Pisces be rising in a Navamsa of a benefic, it leads to bliss', highlighting the importance of Navamsa Lagna and its lord's nature.

rules.rule_name: 8th House Influence Hierarchy: Occupants/Aspects vs. Ruler

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the 8th house for disease or cause of death.
check_logic.calculation: First, check for planets occupying the 8th house. Second, check for planets aspecting the 8th house. If neither are present, then consider the ruler of the 8th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the primary planetary influence on the 8th house, leading to flawed predictions regarding disease or cause of death.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from: 'If planets are not posited in the 8th house or are not aspecting it, the 8th house causes diseases as above according to the nature of the ruler of that house.'

check_logic.condition: When multiple planets occupy or aspect the 8th house, or when comparing the influence of occupants/aspects with the ruler.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of all planets influencing the 8th house (occupants, aspectors, ruler if applicable) and identify the strongest one. The strongest planet's effects will be most prominent.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify the strongest planet will lead to misattribution of primary effects related to the 8th house, resulting in inaccurate predictions.

rules.rule_name: Stronger of Sun or Moon for Past Birth Analysis

check_logic.condition: When determining the nature of the previous birth.
check_logic.calculation: Compare the strength of the Sun and the Moon using standard strength assessment methods (e.g., Shadbala components, positional strength).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the primary indicator for past birth, leading to erroneous past birth analysis.

rules.rule_name: Past Birth Origin - Decanate Ruler Jupiter

rules.rule_name: Past Birth Rank - Decanate Ruler in Exaltation

rules.rule_name: Past Birth Rank - Decanate Ruler Fallen Off Exaltation

check_logic.calculation: Identify the decanate occupied by the stronger of the Sun or Moon. Determine if the lord of this decanate has 'fallen off' its exaltation point (i.e., not exalted, but not debilitated, implying average dignity).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction of the previous birth's rank (mediocre).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of a set of rules for past birth rank. 'Fallen off therefrom' implies average dignity.

rules.rule_name: Past Birth Rank - Decanate Ruler in Depression

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction of the previous birth's rank (low).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 1 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of a set of rules for past birth rank.

rules.rule_name: No Full Aspects Between Sun, Mercury, and Venus

check_logic.condition: When assessing mutual aspects between the Sun, Mercury, and Venus.
check_logic.calculation: Verify that the Sun, Mercury, and Venus do not form full aspects with each other. Only partial aspects, implying limited orbs, are possible between these planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary relationships and their mutual influence, leading to erroneous interpretations of chart dynamics.
rules.classical_reference: Saravali (implied from discussion of Kalyana Varma's views on aspects)
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from an inference about Kalyana Varma's teaching on aspects, explicitly stating 'Full aspects cannot be extended between these planets' for Sun, Mercury, and Venus.

check_logic.condition: Before commencing any astrological analysis or prediction for an individual.
check_logic.calculation: Verify the availability of accurate birth time (date, time, and place) to construct a reliable horoscope (birth chart).

rules.impact_if_violated: Without accurate birth time and a resulting horoscope, astrological analysis and predictions are deemed unreliable, inaccurate, or impossible, akin to describing an incision in wood or a book.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is derived from Narada's statement in Saravali, emphasizing the fundamental necessity of a horoscope (derived from birth time) for any meaningful astrological prediction.

rules.rule_name: Divisional Charts (Vargas) are essential for prediction

check_logic.condition: When making any astrological prediction regarding specific areas of life.
check_logic.calculation: Ensure relevant divisional charts (e.g., D9 for marriage, D10 for career, D7 for children) are analyzed in conjunction with the Rashi (D1) chart to confirm and refine the promise of the horoscope.

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions will be incomplete, inaccurate, or lack depth without considering the specific divisional chart relevant to the area of life being analyzed.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted directly from the section title: 'SUB-DIVISIONS ESSENTIAL: The effects of a horoscope should be predicted according to the divisions of houses'.

rules.rule_name: Divisional Charts are Essential for Prediction

check_logic.condition: When making any astrological prediction regarding the effects of a horoscope
check_logic.calculation: Analyze relevant divisional charts (e.g., D9 for marriage, D10 for career, D7 for children) in conjunction with the Rashi (D1) chart to confirm and refine predictions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions will be incomplete, inaccurate, or lack depth and specificity without considering divisional charts.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the section title '12. SUB-DIVISIONS ESSENTIAL: The effects of a horoscope should be predicted according to the divisions of houses'.

check_logic.condition: Before making any astrological prediction based on a horoscope.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength (e.g., dignity, Shadbala, Avasthas) of the lords of the relevant divisional houses (e.g., D9 for marriage, D10 for career, D7 for children) pertaining to the specific area of life being analyzed.

rules.impact_if_violated: It is impossible to make accurate or complete astrological predictions, as the fundamental strengths and weaknesses related to specific life areas are not understood.
rules.extraction_notes: The text explicitly states that 'Without knowing the strength of such lords, one cannot lay even a step forward in the direction of astrology', making this a non-negotiable foundational rule.

rules.rule_name: Sun's strength is essential for Yoga efficacy

check_logic.condition: When analyzing any Yoga in the horoscope
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Sun (e.g., Shadbala, dignity, house placement, aspects). The Sun must be strong.

rules.impact_if_violated: Other Yogas in the horoscope will not become efficacious or will have significantly reduced efficacy for the native.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from the note 'the strength of the Sun is very much essential in order that other Yogas become efficacious for the native.'

rules.rule_name: Weak Sun or Sun in 7th house reduces efficacy of other planets

check_logic.condition: Sun is in the 7th house OR Sun is in a weak state (e.g., debilitated, low Shadbala, combust, etc.)
check_logic.calculation: Check Sun's house placement. Check Sun's dignity, Shadbala, combustion status, etc., to determine weakness.

rules.impact_if_violated: The force and positive effects of other heavenly bodies (planets) in the horoscope will be reduced.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from the note 'the Sun in the setting house (7th house) or in a weak state, reduces the force of other heavenly bodies.'

rules.rule_name: Sun in Ascendant bestows creative power

check_logic.condition: Sun is placed in the Ascendant (east) at birth
check_logic.calculation: Check Sun's house placement. If in 1st house, it is considered in the east.

rules.impact_if_violated: Native may lack creative power or have reduced creative abilities.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from the note 'if the Sun, at birth, is on the east, he bestows creative power on the native.'

rules.rule_name: Sun in 10th house indicates high achievements

check_logic.condition: Sun is placed in the 10th house (Zenith) in the birth horoscope
check_logic.calculation: Check Sun's house placement. If in 10th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Native may not reach enviable heights in life or career.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from the note 'when he is in the Zenith in the horoscope of birth (i.e. on the 10th cusp), one is likely to reach enviable heights in life.'

check_logic.condition: To assess the inherent strength and prominence of the native's personality and life path.
check_logic.calculation:
Determine if the natal Lagna (Ascendant) falls within a Vargothama Navamsa. A Vargothama Navamsa is defined as:
1. The first Navamsa (0°00' - 3°20') of a Movable Rasi (Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn).
2. The fifth Navamsa (13°20' - 16°40') of a Fixed Rasi (Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius).
3. The ninth Navamsa (26°40' - 30°00') of a Common Rasi (Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces).

rules.impact_if_violated: If Lagna is not Vargothama, the native may lack the inherent strength, stability, and prominence indicated by this specific divisional dignity, potentially leading to less impactful or recognized efforts in their life's journey.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines a specific state of the Lagna in the Navamsa chart that indicates inherent strength and prominence for the native. It's a fundamental check for assessing the native's potential.

check_logic.condition: When determining the solar month or interpreting calendar-based astrological events.
check_logic.calculation: Verify that the identified solar month (e.g., Mesha Sankranti marks the beginning of the solar month associated with Aries) correctly corresponds to the Sun's transit through the respective Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect solar month identification, leading to errors in calendar-based calculations, festival timings, and general chart interpretation related to solar ingress.
rules.classical_reference: Saravali_Vol1, section 7. SYNONYMS OF RASIS : Kriya, Tavuru, Jutuma, Notes
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the notes section defining solar months as a result of Sun's movement in a particular Rasi.

check_logic.condition: When identifying or referring to zodiac signs in any astrological calculation or interpretation.
check_logic.calculation: Ensure that the 12 Rasis are correctly identified by their classical Sanskrit names as listed: Mesha, Vrishabha, Mithuna, Karkata, Simha, Kanya, Tula, Vrischika, Dhanvi, Makara, Kumbha, Meena.

rules.impact_if_violated: Fundamental errors in identifying zodiac signs, leading to incorrect house placements, planetary dignities, and all subsequent astrological calculations and interpretations.
rules.classical_reference: Saravali_Vol1, section 7. SYNONYMS OF RASIS : Kriya, Tavuru, Jutuma, verse ३
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the verse listing the 12 Rasi names. This rule validates the correct mapping of sign names.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the lord of the first decanate (0-10 degrees) of any Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: The lord of the first decanate is the lord of the Rasi itself.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planet's strength and influence in the D3 chart, leading to erroneous predictions regarding siblings, courage, and general well-being.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for calculating the lord of the first decanate (D3).

check_logic.condition: When calculating the lord of the second decanate (10-20 degrees) of any Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: The lord of the second decanate is the lord of the 5th Rasi from the Rasi in question.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planet's strength and influence in the D3 chart, leading to erroneous predictions regarding siblings, courage, and general well-being.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for calculating the lord of the second decanate (D3).

check_logic.condition: When calculating the lord of the third decanate (20-30 degrees) of any Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: The lord of the third decanate is the lord of the 9th Rasi from the Rasi in question.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the planet's strength and influence in the D3 chart, leading to erroneous predictions regarding siblings, courage, and general well-being.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for calculating the lord of the third decanate (D3).

rules.rule_name: Planetary influence on Kalapurusha limb strength

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength or weakness of a specific body part based on the Kalapurusha scheme.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the sign corresponding to the body part using the Kalapurusha Limb Mapping. 2. If a benefic planet is placed in that sign, the limb will be strong. 3. If a malefic planet is placed in that sign, the limb will be weak.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the inherent strength or weakness of a body part.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the direct statement in Verse 6 regarding benefic/malefic influence on Kalapurusha limbs.

rules.rule_name: Planetary influence on individual's limb strength (Lagna-based)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength or weakness of a specific body part for an individual.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the house corresponding to the body part by counting from the Natal Lagna (1st house for head, 2nd for face, etc.). 2. If a benefic planet is placed in that house/sign, the limb will be strong. 3. If a malefic planet is placed in that house/sign, the limb will be weak. This assessment must be done in addition to the Kalapurusha assessment.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete or incorrect assessment of the inherent strength or weakness of a body part for an individual.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the 'Notes' section of Verse 6, emphasizing the need to consider Natal Lagna in addition to Kalapurusha for individual limb assessment.

check_logic.calculation: Each Rasi is divided into nine Navamsas, each of 3 degrees and 20 minutes (3° 20').

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Saptamsa (D7) chart for analysis of progeny, relationships, and creative endeavors.
check_logic.calculation: Each Rasi is divided into 7 equal parts (4° 17' 8.57" each). The counting of Saptamsa for odd signs starts from the sign itself. For even Rasis, the counting starts from the 7th sign (opposite house) from the Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Saptamsa chart, leading to erroneous analysis of progeny, relationships, and creative endeavors.
rules.extraction_notes: Describes the method for calculating Saptamsa divisions and their starting points based on odd/even signs.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Turyamsa (D4) chart for analysis of happiness, property, and mother.
check_logic.calculation: Each Rasi is divided into 4 equal parts (7° 30' each). The lords of the 4 Turyamsas are: 1st by the lord of the same Rasi, 2nd by the lord of the 4th sign from it, 3rd by the lord of the 7th sign from it, and 4th by the lord of the 10th sign from it.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Turyamsa chart, leading to erroneous analysis of happiness, property, and mother.
rules.extraction_notes: Describes the method for calculating Turyamsa divisions and their lords.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Dasamsa (D10) chart for analysis of career, profession, and status.
check_logic.calculation: Each Rasi is divided into 10 equal parts (3° each). For odd signs, the counting of Dasamsa starts from the sign itself. For even signs, the counting starts from the 9th sign from the Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dasamsa chart, leading to erroneous analysis of career, profession, and status.
rules.extraction_notes: Describes the method for calculating Dasamsa divisions and their starting points based on odd/even signs.

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Shodasamsa (D16) chart for analysis of vehicles, happiness, and general well-being.
check_logic.calculation: Each Rasi is divided into 16 equal parts (1° 52' 30" each). The counting commences from Aries for Movable (Chara) Rasis, from Leo for Fixed (Sthira) Rasis, and from Sagittarius for Dual (Dwiswabhava) Rasis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Shodasamsa chart, leading to erroneous analysis of vehicles, happiness, and general well-being.
rules.extraction_notes: Describes the method for calculating Shodasamsa divisions and their starting points based on Rasi type (Chara, Sthira, Dwiswabhava).

check_logic.condition: When calculating the Vimsamsa (D20) chart for analysis of spiritual progress, worship, and religious inclinations.
check_logic.calculation: Each Rasi is divided into 20 equal parts (1° 30' each). The counting commences from Aries for Movable (Chara) signs, from Sagittarius for Dual (Dwiswabhava) signs, and from Leo for Fixed (Sthira) signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Vimsamsa chart, leading to erroneous analysis of spiritual progress, worship, and religious inclinations.
rules.extraction_notes: Describes the method for calculating Vimsamsa divisions and their starting points based on Rasi type (Movable, Dual, Fixed). The text uses 'common signs' which is interpreted as 'Fixed (Sthira) signs' to cover all three Rasi types (Chara, Sthira, Dwiswabhava) distinctly.

check_logic.condition: When defining or calculating the angular extent of a Rasi (sign).
check_logic.calculation: Verify that one Rasi (sign) is defined as 30 degrees of arc, which is equivalent to 1800 Kalas (minutes of arc).

rules.impact_if_violated: All astrological calculations, including planetary positions, house cusps, and divisional chart placements, will be fundamentally incorrect.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the fundamental angular measure of a Rasi (sign) in the zodiac.

check_logic.condition: When defining or calculating the total number of divisional units (Horas, Drekkana, Navamsas) across the entire zodiac.
check_logic.calculation: Verify that the total number of Horas in the zodiac is 24 (2 per sign), Drekkana is 36 (3 per sign), and Navamsas is 108 (9 per sign). Also, confirm that (Total Horas + Total Drekkana = 60) and (Total Horas + Total Navamsas = 132).

rules.impact_if_violated: Divisional chart structures and calculations will be incorrect, leading to erroneous analysis of specific life areas.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the total number of Hora, Drekkana, and Navamsa divisions across the 12 signs of the zodiac, which are foundational for constructing divisional charts.

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the natal chart, specifically the positions of Lagna and planets.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the junctions between water and fire signs: Cancer-Leo, Scorpio-Sagittarius, Pisces-Aries. Determine if the Lagna degree or any planet's degree falls within these sensitive zones. The precise degree range for Gandanta (e.g., last 1 degree of water sign and first 1 degree of fire sign, or last 3°20' of water sign and first 3°20' of fire sign) should be applied based on the chosen tradition.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify Lagna or planets in Gandanta can lead to misinterpretation of their inherent strength, stability, and the foundational aspects of the native's life, potentially overlooking significant challenges or instability.
rules.extraction_notes: This verse defines the specific sign junctions that constitute Gandanta ('कुलीरमीनालिगृहान्तसन्धि वदन्ति गण्डान्तमिति प्रसिद्धम्' - 'the junction at the end of the houses of Cancer, Pisces, and Scorpio is known as Gandanta'). The rule is to identify if Lagna or any planet is positioned within these sensitive zones. The exact degree span of Gandanta is a matter of further textual interpretation, but the signs involved are clearly specified here.

check_logic.condition: To determine the fundamental malefic or benefic nature of a Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: Odd Rasis (Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius, Aquarius) are malefic. Even Rasis (Taurus, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, Pisces) are benefic.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Rasi nature will lead to fundamental errors in planetary and house analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the description of Rasi classification, where Aries onwards, alternatively malefic and benefic. The notes section clarifies Aries as odd and Taurus as even, implying odd signs are malefic and even signs are benefic.

check_logic.condition: To determine the fundamental gender of a Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: Odd Rasis (Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius, Aquarius) are male. Even Rasis (Taurus, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, Pisces) are female.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Rasi gender will lead to fundamental errors in planetary and house analysis, especially for progeny and relationship matters.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the description of Rasi classification and explicitly confirmed by the notes section which lists each Rasi's odd/even and male/female nature.

check_logic.condition: To determine the fundamental modality (movable, fixed, dual) of a Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn are Chara (Movable). Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius are Sthira (Fixed). Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces are Dviswabhava (Dual).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Rasi modality will lead to fundamental errors in understanding the nature of houses and planets placed therein.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the description of Rasi classification.

check_logic.condition: To identify if a native is born in Gandanta.
check_logic.calculation: Birth occurring in the ending portions of Cancer, Scorpio, or Pisces Rasis.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify Gandanta will lead to missing a critical birth condition with profound implications for survival, mother's well-being, and progeny.
rules.extraction_notes: The text defines Gandanta by the signs, but does not specify the exact degrees for 'ending portions'. Further research would be needed for precise degree definition.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and potential of a Rasi (sign/house).
check_logic.calculation: A Rasi is considered strong if it is aspected by its own lord, or by a planet that is friendly to its lord, or by Mercury, or by Jupiter. Aspects from planets other than these do not lend strength to the Rasi in this context.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rasi will lack inherent strength, potentially weakening the significations and matters associated with that Rasi and the houses it represents.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies which planetary aspects contribute to the inherent strength of a Rasi. Aspects from planets other than the Rasi lord, its friends, Mercury, or Jupiter do not confer this specific type of strength.

rules.rule_name: Sun and Moon have no Trimsamsa Lordship

check_logic.condition: When determining Trimsamsa (D30) lordship for any planet.
check_logic.calculation: Verify if the planet in question is the Sun or the Moon. If so, they do not rule any Trimsamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrectly assigning a Trimsamsa lord to Sun or Moon, leading to fundamental errors in D30 analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly stated in the notes section of the verse: 'The Sun and the Moon have no lordship over Trimsamsa division.'

check_logic.calculation: If the planet's longitude within the odd sign is between 0°0'0" and 5°0'0" (inclusive of 0°, exclusive of 5°0'1"), its Trimsamsa lord is Mars.

rules.rule_name: Digbala of Human Signs in Lagna

check_logic.condition: When assessing the directional strength (Digbala) of a sign in the Lagna (East).
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign occupying the Lagna (Ascendant) is a Human sign (Gemini, Virgo, Libra, Aquarius, or the first half of Sagittarius). If so, the sign gains directional strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The inherent directional strength of the sign is not correctly assessed, leading to an inaccurate overall strength calculation for planets placed within it.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assessing directional strength (Digbala) of signs. Human signs are: Gemini, Virgo, Libra, Aquarius and first half of Sagittarius.

rules.rule_name: Digbala of Quadruped Signs in 10th House

check_logic.condition: When assessing the directional strength (Digbala) of a sign in the 10th house (South).
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign occupying the 10th house is a Quadruped sign (Aries, Taurus, Leo, the second half of Sagittarius, or the first half of Capricorn). If so, the sign gains directional strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The inherent directional strength of the sign is not correctly assessed, leading to an inaccurate overall strength calculation for planets placed within it.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assessing directional strength (Digbala) of signs. Quadruped signs are: Aries, Taurus, Leo, second half of Sagittarius and first half of Capricorn.

check_logic.condition: When assessing the directional strength (Digbala) of a sign in the 7th house (West).
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign occupying the 7th house is Scorpio. If so, the sign gains directional strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The inherent directional strength of the sign is not correctly assessed, leading to an inaccurate overall strength calculation for planets placed within it.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assessing directional strength (Digbala) of signs. Scorpio is classified as a Keeta Rasi and gains strength in the West.

rules.rule_name: Digbala of Watery Signs (excluding Scorpio) in 4th House

check_logic.condition: When assessing the directional strength (Digbala) of a sign in the 4th house (North).
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the sign occupying the 4th house is a Watery sign (Cancer, Pisces, or the second half of Capricorn, excluding Scorpio). If so, the sign gains directional strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The inherent directional strength of the sign is not correctly assessed, leading to an inaccurate overall strength calculation for planets placed within it.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assessing directional strength (Digbala) of signs. Watery signs are: Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces and second half of Capricorn. The text specifies 'watery signs (excluding Scorpio)' for the 4th house.

rules.rule_name: Kalabala of Quadruped Signs at Night

check_logic.condition: When assessing the temporal strength (Kalabala) of a sign.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the birth time falls during the night. If so, Quadruped signs (Aries, Taurus, Leo, second half of Sagittarius, first half of Capricorn) gain temporal strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The inherent temporal strength of the sign is not correctly assessed, leading to an inaccurate overall strength calculation for planets placed within it.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assessing temporal strength (Kalabala) of signs. Quadruped signs are: Aries, Taurus, Leo, second half of Sagittarius and first half of Capricorn.

rules.rule_name: Kalabala of Human Signs during Day

check_logic.condition: When assessing the temporal strength (Kalabala) of a sign.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the birth time falls during the day. If so, Human/Biped signs (Gemini, Virgo, Libra, Aquarius, first half of Sagittarius) gain temporal strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The inherent temporal strength of the sign is not correctly assessed, leading to an inaccurate overall strength calculation for planets placed within it.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assessing temporal strength (Kalabala) of signs. Human signs are: Gemini, Virgo, Libra, Aquarius and first half of Sagittarius.

rules.rule_name: Kalabala of Sandhya Signs at Daybreak/Nightfall

check_logic.condition: When assessing the temporal strength (Kalabala) of a sign during Sandhya (daybreak or nightfall).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the birth time falls during daybreak or nightfall. If so, the second half of Capricorn, Scorpio, Cancer, and Pisces gain temporal strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The inherent temporal strength of the sign is not correctly assessed, leading to an inaccurate overall strength calculation for planets placed within it.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for assessing temporal strength (Kalabala) of signs. These signs are strong at the time of day break and/or night fall.

check_logic.condition: When determining the cardinal direction associated with a Rasi according to Prasna Marga's system.
check_logic.calculation: If the Rasi is Aquarius or Capricorn, its cardinal direction is North.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect cardinal direction assigned to the Rasi, leading to erroneous predictions for queries involving directions (e.g., travel, lost items, Prasna).
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the cardinal direction for Aquarius and Capricorn as per Prasna Marga's system. It's a fundamental mapping used in specific astrological analyses.

check_logic.condition: Always assess the dignity of each planet.

rules.rule_name: Sign Ascension Type: Short Ascension Signs

check_logic.condition: When analyzing physical characteristics or general constitution based on the Lagna sign.
check_logic.calculation: Verify if the Lagna sign (or any relevant sign for body parts) is classified as a 'short ascension' sign. The signs are: Pisces, Taurus, Aries, Aquarius.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect classification of a sign's ascension type, leading to erroneous predictions regarding physical characteristics (e.g., head shape, body form).
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the signs classified as 'short ascension' which is foundational data for interpreting physical characteristics based on the Lagna.

rules.rule_name: Rasi Exaltation Neutralized by Navamsa Debilitation

check_logic.condition: When a planet is exalted in the Rasi (D1) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the same planet is debilitated in the Navamsa (D9) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Overestimation of planetary strength, leading to inaccurate predictions regarding the planet's ability to deliver good results.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the 'Notes' section, highlighting the importance of divisional chart confirmation for dignity.

rules.rule_name: Rasi Debilitation Improved by Navamsa Exaltation

check_logic.calculation: Check if the same planet is exalted in the Navamsa (D9) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Underestimation of planetary strength, leading to inaccurate predictions regarding the planet's ability to deliver good results despite initial weakness.
